issue_title: [BUG] No thank you page for Apple devices after correct payment
issue_body:
### Describe the bug
Apple users doesn't see Thank you page when they come back to shop after success payment.

### Use Case
When PWA shop has payment as a redirection to 3rd party payment (Service Provider payment page), after success payment he has to be redirected to Thank you page. We discoved that it works corretly for all devices except Apple devices. For Apple devices customer see Thank you page without any content (probably because apple device user lose his context and PWA doesn't display him his details page, because PWA doesn't know which user it is).

### Describe the bug / Provide reproduction steps, example
1. When customer click "Place an order" PWA send request to S6 to save order
2. S6 save order and then send request to Payment Provider to get Payment URL (request has information about ordered products, and successURL/failedURL links as well which redirect to S6 backend). 
3. S6 return Payment URL to PWA, and then PWA redirect customer to Payment Proider page
4. After success payment, Payment provider redirect customer to shop (first to S6 backend, and then backend redirect to PWA thank you page)
5. Finaly Apple devices users see empty Thank you page (other devices users see correct Thank you page) 

### Additional context
- shopware-pwa version: 0.7.2 (probably it will be reproduced in newer versions)
- Shopware 6 detailed version: 6.3.5.1 

### Reproduced on shops
- https://yope.me/
- https://www.vintageria.it/


### Anything additional which you can add
- device information: apple / safari
pr_title: fix(nuxt module): redirect 302 issue on apple devices

issue_title: [Bug] wrong locale for static pages
issue_body:
### What happened?

EDIT: I think this is not only for static pages, but also dynamic pages, see the corresponding PR

I'm on a site setup with different subdomains used for localization. 
I'm on a site with german locale set in the application.
Then I switch to a static page (nuxt page) and the locale suddenly changes to another locale (in my case english).

This happens because of this change here:
https://github.com/vuestorefront/shopware-pwa/commit/01eaf2f9c4d572aa88ab67bacdc1c254011b65d9#diff-76fdfb9d975492f8aceb4679cdd288294f91798f0bd9631358629a764a417be3 

Now not only routes with localization based on the prefixPath get handed the domain config, but all routes, which is fine at first.
But during the routing process, now suddenly `domainConfig` ist set here: 
https://github.com/vuestorefront/shopware-pwa/blob/master/packages/nuxt-module/plugins/domain.js#L144

The comment some lines above states the assumption, that "Only routes with prefixPath have to have the domainConfig added in the meta-object" 
https://github.com/vuestorefront/shopware-pwa/blob/master/packages/nuxt-module/plugins/domain.js#L138 

This of course now breaks, because the assumption isn't valid anymore. Because static-routes always will be the first route from all routes, that matches the new path, the `domainConfig` now will be always the one of the first route in the array matching the path.

This can be solved by also checking, if the new route is a static route, I'll provide a PR asap.

### Steps To Reproduce

_No response_

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the existing issues

### Version

Latest stable (generated by CLI)

### Which element is affected?

nuxt-module

### Environment

```markdown
- Shopware PWA version: 1.0.1
```


### Relevant log output

_No response_
pr_title: fix(nuxt-module): domain configuration now determined properly on route change

issue_title: [Bug] duplicate creation/mount of views
issue_body:
### What happened?

Right now every view (`components/views/*`) gets created and rendered twice. This is a huge problem as the whole subtree i created and mounted twice which also potentially causes duplicate API-calls etc.

Expected is that the view-components and their subtree only get created once per page view.

### Steps To Reproduce
1) Go to a category page
2) Go to a product page
--> for example the cross-sells API-call is duplicated.
If you write a `console.log("created")` into the setup-method of for example `components/views/frontend.detail.page.vue`, it gets even more obvious since it appears twice in the console.

The root of problem is, that the pages-middleware is executing the search-method of useCms which then of course updates the page value. 
This causes the `getComponent` value in `pages/_.vue` to recompute which in turn causes the view-component to be created again. 
Shortly after the current page-instance gets unmounted and destroyed by the router and then a new instance of `_.vue` is created. This causes another creation and mount of the respective view-component.

As I see it, getComponent doesn't have to be a "real" computed value and can be executed in `setup` directly, because the page-value won't update during the lifecycle of its instance. The page-value should already be set on the creation of the _.vue-instance, because the middleware is always executed before route change.
Another option is to use `asyncData` on the page to essentially do the logic that is currently done in the pages-middleware. This would then not mess with the execution order and prevent unneccessary rerenders.

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the existing issues

### Version

Canary (generated by CLI)

### Which element is affected?

default-theme

### Environment

_No response_

### Relevant log output

_No response_
pr_title: fix(theme-base): fix duplicate rendering of cms-page subtree

issue_title: 🤖 🐛 - Login button stuck in loading state when auth popup is closed without completing login
issue_body:
### Describe the issue that you're seeing. Any videos (eg. YouTube or Loom) or screenshots help!

When clicking the "Log In" button in the TinaCMS admin panel, an authentication popup opens. If the user closes this popup without completing the login flow (either by clicking outside, pressing Escape, or using the close button), the login button remains in a loading/spinner state indefinitely.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Navigate to /admin
2. Click the "Log In" button
3. Authentication popup appears
4. Close the popup without completing login (close button, Escape key, or click outside)
5. Observe the login button

Expected Behavior:
The login button should reset to its default state when the popup is closed, allowing the user to attempt login again.

Actual Behavior:
The login button remains in a loading state with a spinner animation that never stops. The user must refresh the page to attempt login again.

Environment:
- TinaCMS version: (run npm list tinacms to get your version)
- Using Tina Cloud authentication
- Next.js

Suggested Fix:
Add an event listener for the popup close/cancel event that resets the button's loading state.

### Reproduction

/admin route with Tina Cloud integrated

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### System Info

```shell
System Info:

- tinacms: 3.3.2
- @tinacms/cli: 2.1.2
- Next.js: 15.3.8
- Node.js: 22.22.0
- npm: 10.9.4
- OS: Windows 11
- Browser: Chrome
```

### Validations

- [x] Follow our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/tinacms/tinacms/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] Read the [docs](https://tina.io/docs).
- [x] Check that there isn't [already an issue](https://github.com/tinacms/tinacms/issues) that reports the same bug.
pr_title: Fix login button stuck in loading state when auth popup is closed

issue_title: Media manager tiles are way too large
issue_body:
### Describe the issue that you're seeing. Any videos (eg. YouTube or Loom) or screenshots help!

On a regular 1920x1080 monitor, I can see a total of 4 columns and 2.5 rows of tiles in the media manager.

I can see that the code was written so that on larger monitors, the media manager's grid view might adapt to 6 or even 9 columns:
https://github.com/tinacms/tinacms/blob/3ded944487d893b53dd672240fd24978335040af/packages/tinacms/src/toolkit/components/media/media-manager.tsx#L476

But even if I zoom out, I still only see only 4 columns.

I suspect this is because the tailwind configuration never even defines the 2xl, 4xl, or 6xl screen sizes that this particular grid makes use of, and tailwind itself only defines [breakpoints](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/responsive-design) up to 2xl / 1536px. -- though, looking through Git blame, I can see https://github.com/tinacms/tinacms/pull/4119/files#diff-78421bfdaed7860cfed1b864d9d38c17242919f6376dd9fcc54a01a7e7679654L419-R419 apparently removing previously-good [container size queries](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/responsive-design#container-size-reference) and replacing them with screen size queries.

### Reproduction

https://github.com/tinacms/tinacms/tree/3ded944487d893b53dd672240fd24978335040af/examples/tina-self-hosted-demo

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open a Tina application, e.g. the self-hosted demo
2. Navigate to a page, then open the Media Manager from the sidebar.
3. Observe there are four columns (in the self-hosted demo, the two image tiles should take up half the width of the page) on a monitor wider than 1536px.
4. Zoom out to simulate an even larger monitor.
5. Observe that there are still only four columns.
6. Since all the tiles are squares, this means that as long as you stick to a regular 16:9 screen ratio, you can only ever see 12 tiles at most.

issue_title: Media Manager / Cloudinary integration broken after update to Tina 3.0.x in live mode
issue_body:
After updating Tina from 2.9.5 to 3.0.2, the Cloudinary Media Store integration in the Media Manager stopped working.

Opening the Media Manager in the Tina UI now results in a "500 – Internal Server Error".

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/05936aa4-07f5-479b-8457-1af352a697f1)

The strange thing is that this only happens on the live site (deployed on Netlify). Locally, everything works as expected:

- In local dev mode, the Media Manager works without any issues.
- In a locally built production version (local build + next start), the error also does not occur.
- the issue seems to be related to the serverless environment. 

Below is an excerpt from the Netlify “Function Next.js Server Handler” error logs:

```
Dec 8, 12:45:13 PM: 6ccc8eb3 ERROR  (node:9) Warning: Failed to load the ES module: /var/task/node_modules/next-tinacms-cloudinary/dist/handlers.js. Make sure to set "type": "module" in the nearest package.json file or use the .mjs extension.
(Use `node --trace-warnings ...` to show where the warning was created)

Dec 8, 12:45:27 PM: 54217a72 ERROR   ⨯ Error: Failed to load external module next-tinacms-cloudinary/dist/handlers.js: SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement outside a module
    at Context.externalRequire [as x] (.next/server/chunks/[turbopack]_runtime.js:514:15)
    at module evaluation (.next/server/chunks/[root-of-the-server]__2f2d85d2._.js:1:26324)
    at instantiateModule (.next/server/chunks/[turbopack]_runtime.js:715:9)
    at getOrInstantiateModuleFromParent (.next/server/chunks/[turbopack]_runtime.js:738:12)
    at Context.esmImport [as i] (.next/server/chunks/[turbopack]_runtime.js:228:20)
    at module evaluation (.next/server/chunks/[root-of-the-server]__2f2d85d2._.js:1:36424)
    at instantiateModule (.next/server/chunks/[turbopack]_runtime.js:715:9)
    at instantiateRuntimeModule (.next/server/chunks/[turbopack]_runtime.js:743:12)
    at getOrInstantiateRuntimeModule (.next/server/chunks/[turbopack]_runtime.js:756:12)
    at Object.m (.next/server/chunks/[turbopack]_runtime.js:765:18)
```

Environment:

- TinaCMS version: 3.0.2 (previously working on 2.9.5)
- Deployment: Netlify (issue only occurs on live site / serverless function)
- Local dev: works correctly, error not reproducible
- Local production build: works correctly, error not reproducible
- It is a Next.js 16 (16.0.7) Site with Cloudinary integrated like in https://tina.io/docs/reference/media/external/cloudinary

Can you please look into this issue?

If this is something I can fix with my Netlify config, please let me know.
Any help, ideas, or guidance on how to fix or work around this issue would be greatly appreciated.

### Reproduction

npx create-tina-app@latest

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### System Info

```shell
System:
    OS: Windows 11 10.0.26200
    CPU: (16) x64 AMD Ryzen 9 8945HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics
    Memory: 11.47 GB / 29.78 GB
  Binaries:
    Node: 22.20.0 - C:\Program Files\nodejs\node.EXE
    npm

issue_title: Invalid version ranges for dependencies of `storybook-angular` v1 package
issue_body:
### Please provide the environment you discovered this bug in.

Angular CLI: 20.3.2
Node: 24.8.0
Package Manager: npm 11.6.0
OS: darwin arm64


Angular: 20.3.1
... animations, common, compiler, compiler-cli, core, elements
... forms, localize, platform-browser, platform-browser-dynamic
... router

Package                         Version
---------------------------------------
@angular-devkit/architect       0.2003.7
@angular-devkit/build-angular   20.3.2
@angular-devkit/core            20.3.7
@angular-devkit/schematics      20.3.7
@angular/build                  20.3.2
@angular/cdk                    20.2.4
@angular/cli                    20.3.2
@schematics/angular             20.3.2
rxjs                            7.8.2
typescript                      5.8.3
zone.js                         0.15.1

### Which area/package is the issue in?

storybook-angular

### Description

In the latest stable version of `@analogjs/storybook-angular` package (`v1.22.2`) there are invalid version ranges for `@storybook/builder-vite` dependency ([link](https://github.com/analogjs/analog/blob/v1.22.2/packages/storybook-angular/package.json#L41)) and `@storybook/angular` ([link](https://github.com/analogjs/analog/blob/v1.22.2/packages/storybook-angular/package.json#L44)) and `storybook` ([link](https://github.com/analogjs/analog/blob/v1.22.2/packages/storybook-angular/package.json#L46C6-L46C15)) peer dependencies.

All of them say `>=8.6.0 || ^9.0.0` which results in the installation of the `v10` of all packages as the first part of the semver allows all the versions starting from `8.6.0`. I believe that's not what the initial intention was. 

Replacing it with `^8.6.0 || ^9.0.0` should fix the issue.

### Other information

To reproduce the issue one can simply run `npm i @analogjs/storybook-angular` in an empty directory and inspect the installed versions of the packages using `npm ls @storybook/angular` and `npm ls storybook`.

### I would be willing to submit a PR to fix this issue

- [ ] Yes
- [ ] No
pr_title: fix(storybook-angular): adjust package ranges for 1.x

issue_title: Bug: Race condition in `performCompilation`
issue_body:
### Please provide the environment you discovered this bug in.

I created a file `repro.js` that can be run with `node repro.js --delay-both` it:
- patches `node_modules/@analogjs/vite-plugin-angular/src/lib/angular-vite-plugin.js` to add an artificial delay in some parts of the compilation to trigger the race condition in a deterministic way.
- runs `npm run build`
- restores the patched plugin

https://stackblitz.com/edit/github-aqa3usxg?file=repro.js&preset=node

```bash
# Mode 1: Delay first call after analyzeAsync → client corrupted
node repro.js --with-delay

# Mode 2: Delay second call after analyzeAsync → client corrupted
node repro.js --delay-second

# Mode 3: Delay both calls → BOTH bundles corrupted
node repro.js --delay-both
```

### Which area/package is the issue in?

vite-plugin-angular

### Description

I have an AnalogJS app that sometimes fails at runtime in a totally non-deterministic way: without changing anything in the codebase, It returns an error `JIT compiler unavailable` on all calls. It happens approximately 1 out of 5 times. When it fails, the AOT transformation fails so there are  still `templateUrl` and `__decorate` calls in the output files.

It was really difficult to narrow the issue in a minimal app so I chose to patch node modules to add artificial delays. My real app uses @defer, server only dependency and dynamically loaded client dependencies.

## In `vite-plugin-nitro.js`

When `@analogjs/vite-plugin-nitro` builds client and SSR environments concurrently via `Promise.all` with `sharedPlugins: true`, the Angular plugin's `performCompilation()` function corrupts the build output. The AOT transformers silently produce no output, leaving raw `templateUrl`/`styleUrl` strings and `__decorate()` calls in the bundle instead of AOT-compiled component definitions.

This is a **data race on shared mutable closure state** — not a timing-dependent flake. The race window is deterministic once two `buildStart` hooks overlap.

## In `angular-vite-plugin.js`

The following variables are **closure-scoped** (shared across all calls) and mutated without synchronization:

| Variable | Line | Mutated at |
|---|---|---|
| `builder` | ~95 | L748, L752, L758 |
| `nextProgram` | ~95 | L749 |
| `outputFile` | L95 | L842 |
| `outputFiles` | L96 | L780 |

### The race sequence

1. **Call 1** (client) enters `performCompilation`, creates `angularProgram_1`, sets shared `builder = B1` and `nextProgram = P1` (L744-749), then yields at `await angularCompiler.analyzeAsync()` (L761).

2. **Call 2** (SSR) enters `performCompilation`, creates `angularProgram_2`, **overwrites** `builder = B2` and `nextProgram = P2` (same lines), then yields at `analyzeAsync()`.

3. Whichever call finishes `analyzeAsync()` first proceeds to:
   - Get transformers from **its own** `angularCompiler.prepareEmit().transformers` (L769) — these transformers hold references to its own `NgtscProgram`'s `TraitCompiler` internal `Map<ts.Declaration, ...>`
   - Create `writ
pr_title: fix: concurrent compilation of ssr and client caused corrupted bundles

issue_title: Browser tests fail for external SCSS styleUrl with vite-plugin-angular 2.4.5
issue_body:
### Please provide the environment you discovered this bug in.

```text
@angular/core                          21.2.8
@angular/common                        21.2.8
@angular/compiler                      21.2.8
@angular/compiler-cli                  21.2.8
@angular/platform-browser              21.2.8

@analogjs/vite-plugin-angular          2.4.5
@analogjs/vitest-angular               2.4.5
vite                                   7.3.2
vitest                                 4.1.4
@vitest/browser                        4.1.4
@vitest/browser-playwright             4.1.4
node                                   22
```

Reproduced on:
- Windows 11 (host)
- Linux (Docker, mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v1.59.1-noble)

### Which area/package is the issue in?

vite-plugin-angular

### Description

After upgrading `@analogjs/vite-plugin-angular` and `@analogjs/vitest-angular` from `2.3.1` to `2.4.5`, browser tests for Angular components that declare an **external** `styleUrl` to a `.scss` file fail during module load.

Unit tests for the same components under `happy-dom` (Vitest node path) pass with the same plugin configuration. Only the Vitest browser module-runner path is affected. Also tests do not fail if the component uses inline `styles: [...]` instead of an external `styleUrl` pointing to `.scss`. So the regression appears to be specific to the external style resource path rather than browser mode in general.

### Minimal reproduction

[Github Repo](https://github.com/kSherp/analog-repro)

A standalone project outside of any monorepo reproduces the bug. Validated results:

- fails on Windows 11 with `@analogjs/vite-plugin-angular@2.4.5`
- fails on Linux (Docker, `mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v1.59.1-noble`) with
  `@analogjs/vite-plugin-angular@2.4.5`
- passes on Windows 11 after downgrading only
  `@analogjs/vite-plugin-angular` / `@analogjs/vitest-angular` to `2.3.1`
- passes on Linux after the same downgrade

### Please provide the exception or error you saw

issue_title: NG0401 error on the client after building an Astro 6 project
issue_body: ### Please provide the environment you discovered this bug in. Reproduction: https://github.com/eblocha/astro-angular-experiments/tree/astro-6-vite-7 Run `pnpm build` then `pnpm preview` and go to `http://localhost:4321/guides/example`. ### Which area/package is the issue in? astro-angular ### Description I recently updated to Astro 6 with vite 7, and the astro-angular integration is now failing on the client during hydration with NG0401 (missing platform). It's hitting this condition in `@angular/core`: https://github.com/angular/angular/blob/main/packages/core/src/application/create_application.ts#L47 This is what the built code looks like: The `!i` is the `!platformRef` from the source. This seems to suggest that `ngServerMode` is defined as truthy during the client code build, which folds the check to `!platformRef`. When built with Astro 5 and vite 6, this check has been removed (stepping through with a debugger shows platformRef is still undefined here). ### Please provide the exception or error you saw ```true Uncaught (in promise) Error: NG0401 S http://localhost:4321/_astro/common.OwyWT5em.js:3 op http://localhost:4321/_astro/common.OwyWT5em.js:4 he http://localhost:4321/_astro/client.DInVqwOQ.js:1 Se http://localhost:4321/_astro/client.DInVqwOQ.js:1 y http://localhost:4321/guides/example:103 r http://localhost:4321/guides/example:103 common.OwyWT5em.js:3:13394 ``` ### Other information _No response_ ### I would be willing to submit a PR to fix this issue - [x] Yes - [ ] No
pr_title: fix: support astro v6 using vite environment api

issue_title: [@analogjs/vite-plugin-angular] `ENAMETOOLONG` when preprocessing inline CSS
issue_body:
### Please provide the environment you discovered this bug in.

Hello. I have a project which uses `vite-plugin-angular` to run vitest. It also uses Tailwindcss which uses Postcss. Altogether, when these plugins combine with the Angular compiler, they occasionally create this bug where the content of the inline component css is encoded to base64 (by `@angular/build`) and then used as a temporary/virtual file name (by `@analogjs/vite-plugin-angular`) for vitest to reference (in postcss). This triggers an exception where `fs.statSync` from Postcss throws `ENAMETOOLONG` because the encoded content exceeds the linux/macos file name limit of 255 bytes. 

### Which area/package is the issue in?

vite-plugin-angular

### Description

I don't think this particular bug is specific to any one of these projects but rather a confluence of all of them together. It is, however, solvable from this plugin's source, which is how I've fixed it with a patch in my project's repo. 

In `angular-jit-plugin.ts` of `vite-plugin-angular` you use `styleId` directly from `angular-build` which can occasionally exceed 255 bytes and is used directly by postcss for the temporary filename. My proposal is to instead hash this string with `sha256` and use the 16-byte hash as the `styleId` instead. It's a 3-line fix:

```
import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
line 25: const hash = createHash('sha256').update(styleId).digest('hex').slice(0, 16);
line 36: replace ${styleId} with ${hash}
```

If you're worried about it, you could even put the fix behind a configuration flag to allow the previous behavior to send the raw base64 content as the styleId instead of the sha256 content.

Thanks

### Please provide the exception or error you saw

```true
Error: ENAMETOOLONG: name too long, stat 'CiAgI[...truncated...]A==.css'
    at Object.statSync (node:fs:1739:25)
    at Object.Once (node_modules/@tailwindcss/postcss/dist/index.js:10:4612)
    at LazyResult.runAsync (node_modules/postcss/lib/lazy-result.js:293:11)
    at runPostCSS (node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/config.js:30145:19)
    at compilePostCSS (node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/config.js:30129:6)
    at compileCSS (node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/config.js:30059:26)
    at preprocessCSS (node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/config.js:30221:9)
    at LoadPluginContext.load (node_modules/@analogjs/vite-plugin-angular/src/lib/angular-jit-plugin.js:21:38)
    at EnvironmentPluginContainer.load (node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/config.js:28759:19)
    at loadAndTransform (node_modules/vite/dist/node/chunks/config.js:22628:21) {
  errno: -63,
  code: 'ENAMETOOLONG',
  syscall: 'stat',
  path: 'CiAgICAgI[...truncated...]A==.css'
}
CssSyntaxError: [postcss] tailwindcss: /workspace/project/CiAgI[...truncated...]A==.css:1:1: ENAMETOOLONG: name too long, stat 'CiAgI[...truncated...]A==.css'

> 1 | 
    | ^
  2 |       @reference 'tailwindcss';
  3 |       :host {
```

### Other information

_No response_

### I would be willing
pr_title: fix(vite-plugin-angular): hash styleId to prevent filename exceeding max length

issue_title: SCSS from Angular libraries not preprocessed when building Storybook with Vite
issue_body:
### Please provide the environment you discovered this bug in.

Node : v22.15.1
Angular :  
- "@angular/cli": "^21.0.3"
- "@angular/common": "^21.0.5",
Angular AnalogJS : 
- "@analogjs/storybook-angular": "^2.2.0"

Here is my main.ts configuration : 
```
import type { StorybookConfig } from '@analogjs/storybook-angular';
import { UserConfig, mergeConfig } from 'vite';
import viteTsConfigPaths from 'vite-tsconfig-paths';

const config: StorybookConfig = {
  stories: ['../src/**/*.mdx', '../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)'],
  addons: ['@storybook/addon-docs', '@storybook/addon-designs', '@storybook/addon-a11y'],
  framework: {
    name: '@analogjs/storybook-angular',
    options: {},
  },
  staticDirs: ['../src/shared/assets', './assets'],

  async viteFinal(config: UserConfig) {
    return mergeConfig(config, {
      plugins: [viteTsConfigPaths()],
    });
  },
};

export default config;
```

The angular.json part relative to build-storybook : 
```
        "build-storybook": {
          "builder": "@analogjs/storybook-angular:build-storybook",
          "options": {
            "configDir": ".storybook",
            "experimentalZoneless": true,
            "compodoc": true,
            "compodocArgs": [
              "-e",
              "json",
              "-d",
              "."
            ],
            "outputDir": "storybook-static",
            "stylePreprocessorOptions": {
              "loadPaths": [
                "src/shared/styles",
                "src/components/**/"
              ]
            }
          }
        }
```

### Which area/package is the issue in?

storybook-angular

### Description

I recently switched Storybook’s builder from Webpack to Vite in an Angular component libraries monorepo.

**The setup is:**

- Angular
- Multiple libraries with multiple entry points (ng-packages)
- Storybook builder: Vite

**What works**

When running start-storybook:

- Components render correctly
- Styles are applied correctly
- SCSS is properly processed

**The problem**

When running build-storybook (static build):

- Components still render correctly
- SCSS from Angular components is NOT preprocessed
- Raw SCSS is injected directly into the generated assets
- Variables, nesting and mixins remain uncompiled

**Expected behavior**

SCSS should be processed the same way as:

- start-storybook
- ng build
- Storybook with the Webpack builder

**What I tried**

- Custom Vite css.preprocessorOptions
- Custom Vite plugins
- Storybook viteFinal configuration
- Trying to align with Angular CLI / ng-packagr behavior

None of these changes seem to affect how SCSS from Angular libraries is handled during the static build.

**Question**
Is this a known limitation of Storybook + Vite when used with Angular libraries and ng-packagr (secondary entry points)?
Is there a specific configuration required to ensure SCSS preprocessing is applied during build-storybook?
Any guidance or minimal working example would be appreciated.

### Please prov

issue_title: Missing await in storybook-angular preset causes wsToken assertion failure
issue_body:
### Please provide the environment you discovered this bug in.

- `@analogjs/storybook-angular@2.2.3`
- `storybook@10.2.10`
- `@storybook/angular@10.2.10`
- Node 24, pnpm 10

### Which area/package is the issue in?

storybook-angular

### Description

## Bug

The `core` export in `@analogjs/storybook-angular` (`src/lib/preset.js`) calls `PresetCore()` (from `@storybook/angular/preset`) without `await`. Since `PresetCore` is async, the return value is a `Promise` — spreading it (`{ ...presetCore }`) silently drops all resolved properties, including `channelOptions.wsToken`.



### Please provide the exception or error you saw

```true
AssertionError [ERR_ASSERTION]: wsToken is required for securing the server channel
    at storybookDevServer
```

### Other information

## Root Cause

```js
// src/lib/preset.js — line 20
export const core = async (config, options) => {
    const presetCore = PresetCore(config, options); // <-- missing await
    return {
        ...presetCore, // spreads a Promise, not the resolved config
        builder: {
            name: import.meta.resolve('@storybook/builder-vite'),
            options: { ...presetCore.options },
        },
    };
};
```

## Fix

```js
const presetCore = await PresetCore(config, options);
```

## Workaround

pnpm patch adding the missing `await`:

```diff
-    const presetCore = PresetCore(config, options);
+    const presetCore = await PresetCore(config, options);
```


### I would be willing to submit a PR to fix this issue

- [x] Yes
- [ ] No
pr_title: fix(storybook-angular): Missing await in storybook-angular preset causes wsToken assertion failure

issue_title: angularBuilderContext is undefined when running Storybook tests via @storybook/addon-vitest
issue_body:
### Please provide the environment you discovered this bug in.

- @analogjs/storybook-angular: 2.2.1
- - @storybook/addon-vitest: 10.1.11
- - - storybook: 10.1.11
- - - - vitest: 4.0.16
- - - - - @angular/core: 21.0.6
- - - - - - Node: 24.x
- - - - - - - Package manager: pnpm 10.x
- - - - - - - - Monorepo: Nx 22.3.3

Unfortunately I was unable to reproduce this in StackBlitz - it seems to require a more complex project setup with the vitest addon configured.

### Which area/package is the issue in?

storybook-angular

### Description

## Summary

When running Storybook component tests via `@storybook/addon-vitest`, the `angularOptionsPlugin` in `preset.js` crashes because `angularBuilderContext` is undefined.

## Root Cause

When running via Vitest (not Angular builders), there's no `angularBuilderContext` available since Vitest doesn't use Angular's build system. The `transform` function in `angularOptionsPlugin` attempts to access `options.angularBuilderContext.getProjectMetadata()` without checking if `angularBuilderContext` exists first.

## Location

The issue is in `@analogjs/storybook-angular/src/lib/preset.js` around line 116, in the `angularOptionsPlugin` function's `transform` method.

## Suggested Fix

Add a guard at the beginning of the `transform` function to skip transformation when `angularBuilderContext` is not available:

```javascript
async transform(code, id) {
  // Skip transformation when angularBuilderContext is not available
  // (e.g., when running via Vitest instead of Angular builders)
  if (!options.angularBuilderContext) {
    return;
  }
  // ... rest of existing transform logic
}
```

## Workaround

Currently using a pnpm patch to add this guard. Happy to submit a PR with this fix if you'd like!

### Please provide the exception or error you saw

```true
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getProjectMetadata')
    at Object.transform (node_modules/@analogjs/storybook-angular/src/lib/preset.js:116:65)
```

### Other information

If you wish I can provide a PR if the suggested fix sounds okay. If you don't approve I can attempt to find and alternative solution with your assistance and then provide the PR, if you wish.


This issue only occurs when using `@storybook/addon-vitest` to run Storybook stories as Vitest tests. Running Storybook normally (via `ng run app:storybook` or Angular builders) works fine because the builder provides the `angularBuilderContext`.

The issue is that when running via Vitest, there's no Angular builder in the execution chain, so `angularBuilderContext` is never populated.

Note: This is a different issue from #1832, which addressed `process is not defined` errors.

---
*This issue was drafted with assistance from [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code).*

### I would be willing to submit a PR to fix this issue

- [x] Yes
- [ ] No

---  
Since this was mainly written with Claude I personally just wanted to take a moment to tell you, thank you for the great job you are doi
pr_title: fix(storybook): use vite config root when angularBuilderContext unavailable

issue_title: A11y: Elements lose visible focus when using a screenreader and hitting Enter to activate
issue_body:
### Have you read the Contributing Guidelines on issues?

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines on issues](https://github.com/facebook/docusaurus/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#issues).

### Prerequisites

- [x] I'm using the latest version of Docusaurus.
- [x] I have tried the `npm run clear` or `yarn clear` command.
- [x] I have tried `rm -rf node_modules yarn.lock package-lock.json` and re-installing packages.
- [x] I have tried creating a repro with https://new.docusaurus.io.
- [x] I have read the console error message carefully (if applicable).

### Description

When navigating a docusaurus site via keyboard while using a screen reader (e.g. NVDA), tabbing to a button and hitting enter causes visible focus to be lost. This is due to https://github.com/facebook/docusaurus/blob/main/packages/docusaurus-theme-common/src/hooks/useKeyboardNavigation.ts, which removes `navigation-with-keyboard` class when clicking, this works for keyboard navigation while not using a screen reader but when using a screen reader hitting enter instead activates mousedown events.

Adding the following css appears to fix the issue:
```css
body:not(.navigation-with-keyboard) *:not(input):focus-visible {
  outline: auto !important;
}
```

Which as you can see uses the `:focus-visible` selector which keeps the outline even if the element sees a mousedown event it will maintain focus if the element was already focused by tabbing to it. If a user clicks on a button not focused it will not have a visible focus ring.

### Reproducible demo

The live docusaurus site exhibits this behaviour so it can be used.

### Steps to reproduce

1. On windows activate [NVDA](https://www.nvaccess.org/download/)
2. Open your browser to https://docusaurus.io/docs
3. Tab to the `collapse` button beside `Getting Started` in the sidebar
4. Hit the enter key

### Expected behavior

Visible focus should be retained

### Actual behavior

Visible focus is lost

### Your environment

- Public source code: N/A
- Public site URL: https://docusaurus.io/docs
- Docusaurus version used: 3.8.1
- Environment name and version (e.g. Chrome 89, Node.js 16.4): N/A
- Operating system and version (e.g. Ubuntu 20.04.2 LTS): N/A


### Self-service

- [x] I'd be willing to fix this bug myself.
pr_title: fix(a11y): remove `useKeyboardNavigation` hook

issue_title: mdx-loader should propagate class names from directives to admonition component
issue_body:
### Have you read the Contributing Guidelines on issues? - [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines on issues](https://github.com/facebook/docusaurus/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#issues). ### Prerequisites - [x] I'm using the latest version of Docusaurus. - [x] I have tried the `npm run clear` or `yarn clear` command. - [x] I have tried `rm -rf node_modules yarn.lock package-lock.json` and re-installing packages. - [x] I have tried creating a repro with https://new.docusaurus.io. - [x] I have read the console error message carefully (if applicable). ### Description Admonitions are written through mdx directives. Mdx parses passed class names and provides them in the mdast: ```md :::info{.important} ::: ``` gives ```json { "type": "root", "children": [ { "type": "containerDirective", "name": "info", "attributes": { "class": "important" }, "children": [] } ] } ``` I would expect the resulting admonition to include the class name, but it doesen't. I came accross this issue when i wanted to format md-tables inside an admonition with smaller cell padding. Instead of ```md :::info{.small} | a | markdown | | ----- | ------------ | | table | inside | ::: ``` i needed to do ```md :::info
| a | markdown | | ----- | ------------ | | table | inside |
::: ``` which works fine, but clutters the md content. ### Reproducible demo https://stackblitz.com/edit/github-on5zd6ky?file=docs%2Fintro.md ### Steps to reproduce 1. `yarn run start` 2. navigate to the `tutorial intro` where the table example from above is shown, the css is inside custom.css ### Expected behavior directive classes should be propagated. ### Actual behavior directive classes are ignored ### Your environment - Public source code: https://stackblitz.com/edit/github-on5zd6ky?file=docs%2Fintro.md&file=src%2Fcss%2Fcustom.css - Public site URL: - Docusaurus version used: - Environment name and version (e.g. Chrome 89, Node.js 16.4): - Operating system and version (e.g. Ubuntu 20.04.2 LTS): ### Self-service - [x] I'd be willing to fix this bug myself.

issue_title: fix(docs): generated-index link translations use category.label instead of category.key for lookup
issue_body:
### Have you read the Contributing Guidelines on issues?

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines on issues](https://github.com/facebook/docusaurus/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#issues).

### Prerequisites

- [x] I'm using the latest version of Docusaurus.
- [x] I have tried the `npm run clear` or `yarn clear` command.
- [x] I have tried `rm -rf node_modules yarn.lock package-lock.json` and re-installing packages.
- [x] I have tried creating a repro with https://new.docusaurus.io.
- [ ] I have read the console error message carefully (if applicable).

### Description

  `getSidebarTranslationFileContent` in `packages/docusaurus-plugin-content-docs/src/translations.ts`
  generates translation keys for category generated-index links using `category.key ?? category.label`    
  (lines 96, 109, 118).

  But `transformSidebarCategoryLink` in the same file looks up those translations using only
  `category.label` (lines 182, 186), ignoring the `key` attribute entirely.

  Line 200 in the same `translateSidebar` function already correctly uses `item.key ?? item.label` for    
  category label lookups. Same for links (line 210) and docs (line 219). Only
  `transformSidebarCategoryLink` was missed.

  Related: #8118

### Reproducible demo

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

  1. Create a sidebar category with a custom `key` attribute in `_category_.json`
  2. Give that category a `generated-index` link with a title and description
  3. Run `docusaurus write-translations` to generate the translation file
  4. Translate the generated-index title and description entries in the translation file
  5. Build the site with the target locale

### Expected behavior

The generated-index page title and description resolve from the translation file using the key `sidebar <name>.category.<key>.link.generated-index.title`.

### Actual behavior

  The generated-index page title and description remain untranslated because `transformSidebarCategoryLink` looks up `sidebar.<name>.category.<label>.link.generated-index.title` while the translation file contains `sidebar.<name>.category.<key>.link.generated-index.title`.

### Your environment

- Public source code:
- Public site URL:
- Docusaurus version used:
- Environment name and version (e.g. Chrome 89, Node.js 16.4):
- Operating system and version (e.g. Ubuntu 20.04.2 LTS):


### Self-service

- [x] I'd be willing to fix this bug myself.
pr_title: fix(content-docs): use category key for generated-index translation lookup

issue_title: CopyButton throws "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'writeText')" in non-HTTPS environments after 3.9.x
issue_body:
### Have you read the Contributing Guidelines on issues?

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines on issues](https://github.com/facebook/docusaurus/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#issues).

### Prerequisites

- [x] I'm using the latest version of Docusaurus.
- [x] I have tried the `npm run clear` or `yarn clear` command.
- [ ] I have tried `rm -rf node_modules yarn.lock package-lock.json` and re-installing packages.
- [ ] I have tried creating a repro with https://new.docusaurus.io.
- [ ] I have read the console error message carefully (if applicable).

### Description

## Bug Report

**Docusaurus version**: 3.9.2
**Node version**: v20.19.5
**Browser**: chrome 145.0.7632.116

### Problem

After upgrading from 3.8.x to 3.9.x, the code block copy button throws an error when the site is accessed via a non-HTTPS local network address (e.g.
`http://192.168.x.x:3000`).

TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'writeText')
    at CopyButton/index.js

### Root Cause

PR #11422 replaced `copy-text-to-clipboard` with the native `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` API. However, `navigator.clipboard` is `undefined` in non-HTTPS contexts
(except `localhost`). The old library had a `<textarea>` fallback that handled this case.

### Reproducible demo

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run `npm run start -- --host 0.0.0.0`
2. Access the site from another device on the same LAN via `http://192.168.x.x:3000`
3. Click the copy button on any code block

### Expected behavior

Code is copied to clipboard successfully.

### Actual behavior

The copy button throws an error and nothing is copied:

TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'writeText')
    at CopyButton/index.js

### Your environment

- Public source code: N/A
- Public site URL: N/A
- Docusaurus version used: 3.9.2
- Environment name and version: Node.js v20.19.5, Chrome 145.0.7632.116
- Operating system and version: Ubuntu 20.04 LTS

### Self-service

- [x] I'd be willing to fix this bug myself.
pr_title: fix(theme): restore copy-text-to-clipboard as lazy fallback for non-secure contexts

issue_title: Pass custom transformSearchClient to a swizzled but Wrapped SearchBar
issue_body:
### Have you read the Contributing Guidelines on issues?

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines on issues](https://github.com/facebook/docusaurus/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#issues).

### Prerequisites

- [x] I'm using the latest version of Docusaurus.
- [x] I have tried the `npm run clear` or `yarn clear` command.
- [x] I have tried `rm -rf node_modules yarn.lock package-lock.json` and re-installing packages.
- [x] I have tried creating a repro with https://new.docusaurus.io.
- [ ] I have read the console error message carefully (if applicable).

### Description

I'm trying to add custom search tracking to the SearchBar by extending the `transformSearchClient` function in `docusaurus.config.ts` but according to https://docusaurus.io/docs/advanced/architecture only serializable values are passed along. So that leaves me with the option to swizzle @docusaurus/theme-search-algolia.

I've swizzled @docusaurus/theme-search-algolia with the command `npm run swizzle @docusaurus/theme-search-algolia` and then I choose to `Wrap` the component. Unfortunately there is no way to pass `transformSearchClient` because this https://github.com/facebook/docusaurus/blob/main/packages/docusaurus-theme-search-algolia/src/theme/SearchBar/index.tsx#L315 doesn't pass props to the `DocSearch` component.

I think it should be possible to pass a custom `transformSearchClient` to a wrapped `SearchBar` component because that would allow for fewer breaking changes moving forward.

### Reproducible demo

https://stackblitz.com/edit/github-pyvgb77t?file=src%2Ftheme%2FSearchBar%2Findex.tsx

### Steps to reproduce

1. add algolia search config
2. swizzle @docusaurus/theme-search-algolia and choose to Wrap the component

### Expected behavior

Be able to pass `transformSearchClient` as props to SearchBar

### Actual behavior

Passed props are ignored

### Your environment

- Public source code:
- Public site URL:
- Docusaurus version used:
- Environment name and version (e.g. Chrome 89, Node.js 16.4):
- Operating system and version (e.g. Ubuntu 20.04.2 LTS):


### Self-service

- [x] I'd be willing to fix this bug myself.
pr_title: feat(theme-search-algolia): allow overriding transformSearchClient

issue_title: Deprecation warning on `experiments.lazyBarrel` in Rspack 1.7+ in `@docusaurus/faster`
issue_body:
### Have you read the Contributing Guidelines on issues?

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines on issues](https://github.com/facebook/docusaurus/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#issues).

### Prerequisites

- [x] I'm using the latest version of Docusaurus.
- [ ] I have tried the `npm run clear` or `yarn clear` command.
- [ ] I have tried `rm -rf node_modules yarn.lock package-lock.json` and re-installing packages.
- [ ] I have tried creating a repro with https://new.docusaurus.io.
- [x] I have read the console error message carefully (if applicable).

### Description

https://github.com/facebook/docusaurus/blob/df259ddb269153e652f3731ab5cd52d12244b45f/packages/docusaurus/src/webpack/base.ts#L175-L182

This property is deprecated by `packages/rspack/src/config/normalization.ts` in Rspack:

https://github.com/web-infra-dev/rspack/commit/ff4d368956a296062cbbc4e7411692de1e2caba2#diff-78648f723799beccc94c4b2e506997d12d1ace8216aeb31c0731c37a6dce024a

```ts
			if (experiments.lazyBarrel) {
				util.deprecate(
					() => {},
					"`experiments.lazyBarrel` config has been deprecated and will be removed in Rspack v2.0. Lazy barrel is already stable and enabled by default. Please remove this option from your Rspack configuration."
				)();
			}
```

https://github.com/web-infra-dev/rspack/blob/ff4d368956a296062cbbc4e7411692de1e2caba2/packages/rspack/src/config/normalization.ts#L369-L374

and is going to be inaccessible by Rspack 2:

https://github.com/web-infra-dev/rspack/pull/12724/changes#diff-78648f723799beccc94c4b2e506997d12d1ace8216aeb31c0731c37a6dce024a

We had better remove it until Rspack 2 is released.

### Reproducible demo

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable `@docusaurus/faster`
2. `docusaurus start` or `docusaurus build`

### Expected behavior

No deprecation warnings

### Actual behavior

```
[Rspack Deprecation] `experiments.lazyBarrel` config is deprecated and will be removed in Rspack v2.0. Lazy barrel is already stable and enabled by default. Remove this option from your Rspack configuration.
```

### Your environment

- Public source code:
- Public site URL: 
- Docusaurus version used: 3.9.2
- Environment name and version (e.g. Chrome 89, Node.js 16.4): Node 24.13.0
- Operating system and version (e.g. Ubuntu 20.04.2 LTS): Windows 25H2

`@rspack/core`: 1.7.3

Affected on 1.7+: https://github.com/web-infra-dev/rspack/commit/ff4d368956a296062cbbc4e7411692de1e2caba2

### Self-service

- [ ] I'd be willing to fix this bug myself.
pr_title: fix(core): Remove deprecated experiments.lazyBarrel config for RsPack

### Reproduction URL

https://github.com/th3f0r3ign3r/authjs-issue.git

### Describe the issue

I have setting up Auth.js with Github Oauth and I decided to add PrismaAdapter with (mongodb) to save user account information. Without adding the adapter everything working great. But when I added the Adapter to `@/auth` it crash

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/81f7eeb0-b76b-4f39-8451-6324828bd2a9)

3. Create a MongoBD account and get a connection string

5. Go to `src/auth/index.ts`comment the adapters line
6. Launch the project `clear && npm run dev` don’t forget to run `npm i`
7. Click on the `Signin with GitHub`
8. After being logged signOut
9. Uncomment the adapter line
10. Refresh your browser

If you follow all these steps normally you will have the same error

Normally they should not have any error. Instead I am doing something wrong but I follow the guide line per line. 😩😩😩

issue_title: @auth/core AuthError.type shouldn't be internal
issue_body:
### Environment

```
  System:
    OS: macOS 15.4.1
    CPU: (10) arm64 Apple M1 Pro
    Memory: 95.63 MB / 16.00 GB
    Shell: 5.9 - /bin/zsh
  Binaries:
    Node: 22.15.0 - ~/.asdf/installs/nodejs/22.15.0/bin/node
    npm: 10.9.2 - ~/.asdf/installs/nodejs/22.15.0/bin/npm
    pnpm: 10.9.0 - ~/.asdf/installs/nodejs/22.15.0/bin/pnpm
  Browsers:
    Chrome: 135.0.7049.115
    Safari: 18.4
  npmPackages:
    @auth/core: ^0.39.0 => 0.39.0 
    @auth/prisma-adapter: ^2.9.0 => 2.9.0 
    next: ^15.3.1 => 15.3.1 
    next-auth: 5.0.0-beta.27 => 5.0.0-beta.27 
    react: ^19.1.0 => 19.1.0
```


### Reproduction URL

https://github.com/CHC383/authjs-autherror-type-bug

### Describe the issue

[@auth/core AuthError.type](https://github.com/nextauthjs/next-auth/blob/12665208ff57bd9be061e7c770c04e476f7ca059/packages/core/src/errors.ts#L44-L47) was marked as internal since next-auth v5.0.0-beta.27 (@auth/core v0.39.0), but it is needed for the custom SignIn page to detect the error type and show messages accordingly, just as shown in the [doc](https://github.com/nextauthjs/next-auth/blob/12665208ff57bd9be061e7c770c04e476f7ca059/docs/pages/guides/pages/signin.mdx) as well as the [built-in SignIn page](https://github.com/nextauthjs/next-auth/blob/12665208ff57bd9be061e7c770c04e476f7ca059/packages/core/src/lib/pages/signin.tsx#L68).

Furthermore, it would be helpful if the [error type to error message mapping](https://github.com/nextauthjs/next-auth/blob/main/packages/core/src/lib/pages/signin.tsx#L8) could be exported, so that users with custom SignIn page could reuse it.

### How to reproduce

`pnpm build`

### Expected behavior

Code built without error
pr_title: fix(core): AuthError.type should not be internal

issue_title: Delete button visibility + missing bulk delete option in Programming Exercises
issue_body: **Describe the bug** In the **Edit Programming Exercise** modal, the **Delete** button is only visible when hovering over the actions area (near **Test this Exercise / Check Submission / Save**). When not hovering, the Delete option is hidden, making it easy to miss and difficult to find. Also, in the **Programming Exercises** list view, there is **no option to select multiple exercises and delete them in bulk**. Users must open/delete items individually, which is inefficient when cleaning up many exercises. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Go to **Learning → Programming Exercises** 2. Click an exercise card (e.g., “Test Divide”) to open **Edit Programming Exercise** modal 3. Look at the bottom action bar without hovering the mouse over it 4. Notice the **Delete** button is not visible 5. Hover over the action buttons area (near **Test this Exercise / Check Submission / Save**) 6. See the **Delete** button appear only on hover 7. Go back to the **Programming Exercises** list page 8. Try selecting multiple exercises for deletion 9. Observe there is **no multi-select / bulk delete** option available **Expected behavior** * The **Delete** button should be consistently visible (not hover-dependent), or placed in a clear and discoverable location. * The list page should support **bulk actions**, such as selecting multiple exercises via checkboxes and deleting them at once (with confirmation). **Screenshots** Attached screenshots show: * Delete button only appearing on hover within the Edit Programming Exercise modal * No bulk selection / delete option on Programming Exercises list page **Desktop (please complete the following information):** * OS: Ubuntu **Additional context** This impacts usability, especially for admins/creators managing many programming exercises. A consistent Delete control and bulk delete support would significantly improve content management.

issue_body:
Bug Report: LMS Sidebar Navigation - Enrollment Menu Unresponsive
Description
The "Enrollment" menu item in the LMS sidebar is failing to trigger the expected navigation. When clicking on the menu link, the application does not redirect to the LMS Enrollment list view, effectively locking the user out of that module via the sidebar.

Steps to Reproduce
Log in to the Frappe LMS desk.

Navigate to the LMS module from the sidebar.

Click on various menu items (e.g., Evaluation, Users, Course) to confirm they work.

Attempt to click on the Enrollments menu item.

Expected Result
Clicking "Enrollments" should navigate the user to the /app/lms-enrollment list view.

Actual Result
The "Enrollments" menu item remains unresponsive. The UI does not navigate, and in some instances, the hover/active state does not trigger correctly compared to other menu items.

Environment Details
Platform: Frappe LMS

Browser: Chrome (as seen in the video)

Date: March 31, 2026

Evidence
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8935f2f6-1995-45d6-b16b-0c085ddd8dcd

issue_title: “Add Lesson” button redirects to previous lesson instead of creating a new one
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
When I delete an existing lesson and immediately attempt to add a new one, the system does not present a blank form. Instead, it seems to retain the state of the deleted lesson or the previous active lesson, causing the "Add Lesson" action to open the editor for the item that was just removed (or the last active item) rather than creating a new entry.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create a new course
2. Click Save
3. Click Add chapter, name Introduction
4. Click Add Lesson, give name Item 1
5. Click Save
6. Go back to course
7. Click Add Lesson, give name Item 2
8. Delete Item 1 Lesson.
9. Click Add Lesson

**Expected behavior**
The editor opens populated with the context/data of different lesson.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Windows 11 Pro
 - Browser Firefox
 - Version 147

**Additional context**
Cross-Browser Check: The issue persists across different browsers and devices, including Chrome, Android Edge, and Android Edge PWA, suggesting this is not a browser-specific client-side issue.

Logs: As this is a self-hosted Docker deployment, I can provide specific container logs if needed to diagnose the state management issue. Please let me know which container or log files would be most helpful.

Please see the video attached here of the issue
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b7457b1c-2923-4ccd-aa7a-4c09685eabf1

```
bench version
erpnext 15.94.0
frappe 15.95.0
hrms 15.54.3
lms 2.44.0
```

issue_title: [Bug] Course and Batch filters ("Enrolled") fail in non-English languages due to hardcoded string
issue_body: ### Issue Body: ### 1. Describe the bug When using Frappe LMS in a language other than English (e.g., Spanish), the "Enrolled" filter on both the /courses and /batches pages does not work as expected. Instead of filtering the list to show only the items the user is enrolled in, the filter is ignored, and a default list (e.g., all published items) is displayed. The root cause is a hardcoded string comparison in the Vue component's logic. The code compares the reactive tab's value against the English string "Enrolled", but in other languages, the tab's value is the translated string (e.g., "Inscritos"), causing the comparison to fail. I have confirmed this bug exists in stable versions v2.29, v2.30, and the current development branch (v2.31.0-dev). ### 2. To Reproduce Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Set up a Frappe LMS instance and set the system's primary language to Spanish (es). 2. Log in as any user (Student, Instructor, etc.). 3. Enroll the user in at least one course and one batch. 4. Navigate to the Courses page or Batch page (/courses, /batches). 5. Click on the "Inscritos" (Enrolled) tab. 6. Expected behavior: The list should be filtered to show only the courses or batch the user is enrolled in. 7. Actual behavior: The filter is not applied. The API request is sent with default filters (e.g., published: 1). 8. Repeat steps 4-7 for the Batches page (/batches). The same issue occurs. ### 3. Evidence and Analysis Debugging in a Spanish Environment (Bug Present): When clicking the "Inscritos" tab, a console.log on the currentTab ref shows its value is the translated string: `// Output in browser console El valor de currentTab es: Inscritos` This causes the conditional logic in updateTabFilter to fail, as it expects the English string. `// From CourseList.vue / BatchList.vue // This comparison fails in Spanish: "Inscritos" == "Enrolled" is false if (currentTab.value == 'Enrolled' && user.data?.is_student) { // This code block is never reached filters.value['enrolled'] = 1; }` As a result, the payload sent to the API is incorrect. Incorrect Payload (Spanish): `{ "filters": { "published": 1 }, ... }` ### Comparison with an English Environment (Works Correctly): In an English environment, the currentTab value is "Enrolled", the comparison succeeds, and the correct payload is sent. Correct Payload (English): `{ "filters": { "enrolled": 1 }, ... }` ### 4. Suggested Solution Change the tab definition from this: `// Current implementation tabs.push({
pr_title: fix: filter tabs not working in non-english languages

issue_title: LMS Certificate Email Template: Inconsistent Variable Naming & Missing Context
issue_body:
## Bug: Inconsistent and Incomplete Template Variables in LMS Certificate Email

**Frappe LMS Version:** 2.44.0
**Frappe Version:** 15.91.2

---

### Describe the Bug

In `LMSCertificate.send_mail()`, template variables are inconsistently named and incomplete, causing broken email templates:

- `member_name` is passed as `student_name` (mismatches the DocType field name)
- `batch_title` is not passed at all
- The full document (`doc`) is not exposed, blocking access to standard and custom fields

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to **LMS Certificate** and create a certificate with **Member Name**, **Batch Name**, and **Batch Title** populated
2. Set a custom email template under **LMS Settings → Certification Template** using `{{ member_name }}` and `{{ batch_title }}`
3. Trigger certificate creation (email sends automatically)
4. Check the received email

### Current Behavior

| Variable | Result |
|---|---|
| `{{ member_name }}` | ❌ Does not render |
| `{{ student_name }}` | ✅ Renders member name (unexpected alias) |
| `{{ batch_title }}` | ❌ Does not render |
| Custom fields | ❌ Not accessible |

### Expected Behavior

- Variable names should match DocType field names (`member_name`, `batch_title`, etc.)
- `doc` should be available in templates, consistent with standard Frappe Notifications behavior
- All standard and custom fields on `LMS Certificate` should be accessible without backend changes
```jinja
{{ doc.member_name }}
{{ doc.batch_title }}
```

issue_title: Uploaded File Appears Across Multiple Assignments Instead of Being Isolated
issue_body: **Description:** When uploading a file to one assignment (of type PDF, DOC, or IMAGE) in the Web version, the uploaded file correctly appears. However, when navigating to a different assignment, the same uploaded file is displayed, even though no file was uploaded there. **Steps to Reproduce:** Log in to the Web Portal. Go to Course → Assignment (type: PDF). Upload a file (e.g., QA Task – RosarioSIS.pdf). Navigate to a different assignment (e.g., type: DOC or IMAGE). Observe that the same file appears as already uploaded in the new assignment. **Expected Result:** Each assignment should display only its own uploaded file, based on its unique ID or submission record. **Actual Result:** The same file (uploaded to the first assignment) appears in other assignments of different types, without user action. **Severity:** Critical — affects data consistency and user submission integrity. **Priority :** High **Environment:** Platform: Web Google Chrome Module: Course→ Assignment Affected types: PDF, DOC, IMAGE **Attachment** https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9a1a9268-0e75-4b2d-91cd-b2aa564a010c **Possible Root Cause:** Frontend may be caching the same file reference globally instead of per-assignment. Backend may not be mapping the uploaded file to the correct assignment ID. Missing unique key or identifier when fetching file data for each assignment. **Recommendation:** Ensure file uploads are linked using the correct assignment ID. In the API response, confirm that each assignment fetches only its own submission. Clear or revalidate local cache/state when switching between assignments. Add test case for verifying isolation between uploads of different assignment types.

issue_title: jobs: 0 in LMS Settings does not hide Jobs tab from authenticated users
issue_body:
Body:
Describe the bug
When jobs is set to 0 (disabled) in LMS Settings → Sidebar, the Jobs tab remains visible in the sidebar for authenticated users (e.g. LMS Student role). The setting appears to work only for guest users via get_sidebar_settings() which returns early if allow_guest_access is disabled.
Steps to Reproduce

Go to LMS Settings → Sidebar
Uncheck "Jobs"
Save
Log in as a student
Jobs tab is still visible in the sidebar
Student can navigate to /lms/job-openings and view/interact with job listings

Expected behavior
When jobs is unchecked in LMS Settings, the Jobs tab should be hidden from all users including authenticated ones.
Actual behavior
Jobs tab remains visible for authenticated users regardless of the setting. Only guest access is affected by this setting.
Additional context

get_sidebar_settings() in api.py returns [] early if allow_guest_access is False, so the jobs setting is never evaluated for authenticated users
Workaround via Role Permission Manager (removing LMS Student from Job Opportunity and LMS Job Application) blocks creation but does not hide the sidebar item or the page

issue_title: 🚨 User Profile applies only after restart
issue_body:
### Version Used

16.8.3

### Expected Behavior

I am using the (experimental) user profiles which adds a profile selection button in the header bar.

I would expect the profile to switch immediatly when selecting another profile.

### Current Behavior

The profile does not switch immediately, but I must restart to apply it.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Activate "Experimental User Profiles" in the settings
2. Click the new profile header button, then "Manage"
3. Add a second profile
4. Switch between the profiles and observe the content does not change
5. Restart the app and observe the change of content

### Can you reproduce this reliably?

Yes, I can reproduce it reliably.

### Console Output

```shell

```

issue_title: Latest version has broken the insert URL link functionality
issue_body:
## Describe the bug
After the update to the @toast-ui/editor@3.2.0 the dialog for URL link insert fails. 

When started in the debug mode, The debuger raises an exception getCustomAttrs is not defined at line 19255 (in the transpilled code).

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Write a text in the wysiwyg format
2. Insert URL link filled both URL and text
3. The link is not visible in the wysiwyg and window for insert doesn't close
4. The link is inserted - as is visible in the mardown, but complete text is put to the text source code (to the ````txt .... ``` block) 

## Expected behaviour
As before update - simply insert the link

## Screenshots
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

![obrazek](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36728060/185871624-fc64213f-593d-491e-8ea7-1d83db89cd23.png)

The OK button doesn't close the window. 

However, when closed using the Cancel (Zrušit on the image), link is visible - but only in the markdown:
![obrazek](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36728060/185871870-960318cc-2f60-4e3e-ab04-2861fa38e4ff.png)

Unfortunately, save fails with an exception and change is not saved. Doing again and saving without former switching to the markdown passes, but the text is converted to the string code:
![obrazek](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36728060/185872763-458b2589-73ee-4ee1-b70f-6f3bc842a926.png)
 
With the markdown:
![obrazek](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36728060/185872836-5ae5419a-efec-44d4-8976-05c801c84cb2.png)

What's worse, the same problem occurs when I am trying to insert the link directly in the markdowns without using the dialog:
![obrazek](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36728060/185873377-a8790b02-6015-4e3c-af37-9cb17afe92ca.png)

it puts several ~to the code and when debugged it always fails with the error:
![obrazek](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36728060/185873815-2110df59-2f34-4291-be2e-98526c7b00c0.png)

and application hangs up.

## Additional context
The used modules are:
aymine.com@5.56.7 
├── @babel/core@7.18.10
├── @babel/plugin-proposal-class-properties@7.18.6
├── @babel/plugin-proposal-object-rest-spread@7.18.9
├── @babel/preset-env@7.18.10
├── @babel/preset-typescript@7.18.6
├── @rollup/plugin-alias@3.1.9
├── @rollup/plugin-babel@5.3.1
├── @rollup/plugin-commonjs@15.1.0
├── @rollup/plugin-node-resolve@9.0.0
├── @toast-ui/chart@4.4.3
├── @toast-ui/editor@3.2.0
├── check-dependencies@1.1.0
├── composer@4.1.0
├── npm-dts@1.3.12
├── npm@8.18.0
├── rollup-cache@1.2.0
├── rollup-plugin-terser@7.0.2
├── rollup-plugin-typescript2@0.27.3
├── rollup@2.78.1
├── tui-calendar@1.15.3
├── tui-code-snippet@2.3.3
├── tui-color-picker@2.2.8
├── tui-tree@4.0.9
└── typescript@4.7.4

issue_title: Bold and Italic intersection incorrect Markdown
issue_body:
## Describe the bug
If you use bold and italic at the intersection of a word, then the output markdown will be incorrect

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to "Wysiwyg" tab
2. Enter text "boldanditalic"
3. Make phrase "boldand" bold, then phrase "anditalic" italic
4. Go to "Markdown" tab. 
5. Incorrect result is preview. Extra asterisks appeared.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8930952/134575623-3b97fe88-4753-4deb-93dd-244d00fb75bd.png)


## Expected behavior
Markdown will be `**bold*****and***​*italic*` or `**bold*****and***_italic_` and preview:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8930952/134576328-831ca41b-449f-491f-bd8b-e642e4f869f6.png)

Editor version: 3.1.0

issue_title: (Still) Wrong path for font scss in video-js.scss on v8.0.4
issue_body:
### Description

Apparently we met the issue mentioned at #7863 . 

### Reduced test case

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

1. Clone the git repository
2. rum `npm install`
3. run `npm test`. Also, one can run `npm run build` afterwards.

Expected result:  
All should be fine.

Actual result:
scripts show errors.

### Errors

```
[... omitted ...]
> video.js@8.0.4 build:css
> npm-run-all build:css:*


> video.js@8.0.4 build:css:cdn
> sass --load-path='./' --load-path='./node_modules/' --no-source-map src/css/vjs-cdn.scss dist/alt/video-js-cdn.css

Error: Can't find stylesheet to import.
  ╷
5 │ @import "videojs-font/scss/icons";
  │         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  ╵
  src\css\video-js.scss 5:9  @import
  src\css\vjs-cdn.scss 3:9   root stylesheet
ERROR: "build:css:cdn" exited with 65.
ERROR: "build:css" exited with 1.
```

The errors seem the same for test or build.

### What version of Video.js are you using?

8.0.4 up to commit a27ee05

### Video.js plugins used.

None

### What browser(s) including version(s) does this occur with?

None. This is from command line

### What OS(es) and version(s) does this occur with?

Windows 11  
npm 9.3.1  
node v18.14.0

Both from windows command line as well as WSL terminal.

issue_title: Unable to set role of attendees when creating new event
issue_body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Click "+ Event"
2. Add an attendee
3. Click the "..." next to the attendee's name
4. You are unable to select the role of that attendee.


### Expected behavior

You should be able to select the role for an attendee.

### Actual behavior

The radio buttons for the attendee's role are empty

### Calendar app version

6.2.0

### CalDAV-clients used

_No response_

issue_title: Duplicating an event is not assigned to the right calendar
issue_body: ### Steps to reproduce Hello, Since last app updates, duplicating an event is not correctly put on the right calendar as the original one. 1. Duplicate an event 2. Event is created on the first calendar from the calendars list 3. Even is then moved to the right calendar ### Expected behavior Duplicating an event should be done directly on the same calendar as the originating one. ### Actual behavior Duplicating an event is assigned to the first calendar of the calendars list. When saving changes it is moved to the right calendar which lead having 2 activites on the Activity app (cf. screenshot below). issue_title: [Bug] inline-code produces wrong markdown
issue_body: ### Initial checklist - [x] I agree to follow the [code of conduct](https://github.com/Saul-Mirone/milkdown/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) - [x] I searched [issues](hhttps://github.com/Saul-Mirone/milkdown/issues) and [discussions](https://github.com/Saul-Mirone/milkdown/discussions) and couldn’t find anything (or linked relevant results below) ### Affected packages and versions 7.6.1 ### Link to runnable example _No response_ ### Steps to reproduce This can be tested in the Milkdown playground. 1. Enter some text without whitespaces for example "InlineCodeWithBoldAndItalicText`" 2. select the text and transform it in inline code 3. select a word and make it bold or italic --> the produced markdown is wrong, bold and italic is missing 5. edit the markdown --> in the editor, the \`code\` characters are visible, this is unexpected example markdown: ``` `InlineCodeWith``Bold``And``Italic``Text` ``` ### Expected behavior the markdown should be generated correctly with the bold and italic formatting: ``` `InlineCodeWith` **`Bold`** `And` *`Italic`* `Text` ``` editing the markdown should not change the editor in an unexpected way ### Actual behavior In the generated markdown the text formatting is missing but the code is splitted in chunks: ``` `InlineCodeWith``Bold``And``Italic``Text` ``` when you use text **with** whitespaces and select a word, it works correctly, for example "Inline Code With **Bold** And _Italic_ Text" when you use text without whitespaces, the produced markdown is wrong Screenshot: ### Runtime Chrome ### OS macOS ### Build and bundle tools _No response_

issue_title: [Bug] Incorrect table parsing when pasting content from Google Docs in Milkdown
issue_body: ### Initial checklist - [x] I agree to follow the [code of conduct](https://github.com/Saul-Mirone/milkdown/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) - [x] I searched [issues](hhttps://github.com/Saul-Mirone/milkdown/issues) and [discussions](https://github.com/Saul-Mirone/milkdown/discussions) and couldn’t find anything (or linked relevant results below) ### Affected packages and versions latest ### Link to runnable example https://milkdown.dev/playground ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open Google Docs. 2. Create a document containing one or more tables and some normal text. 3. Copy and paste in two scenarios: - Case A: Copy only a table and paste into Milkdown. - Case B: Copy the entire document (including multiple tables and text) and paste into Milkdown. Used latest milkdown playgroud to test ### Expected behavior - Tables should be pasted exactly as copied. - Header rows should not be duplicated. - All tables in the document should be correctly parsed and rendered as Milkdown tables. - Document structure and formatting should remain consistent. ### Actual behavior - When pasting only a table, Milkdown adds an extra row at the top duplicating the header content. - When pasting a full document containing multiple tables, Milkdown fails to parse tables correctly. - Some tables are not rendered as tables and lose their structure or appear as plain text. ### Runtime Chrome ### OS macOS ### Build and bundle tools Next.js

issue_title: [Bug] Extra Blank Lines in Lists on Android Browsers (BulletList, OrderedList, and TaskList)
issue_body:
### Initial checklist

- [x] I agree to follow the [code of conduct](https://github.com/Saul-Mirone/milkdown/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] I searched [issues](hhttps://github.com/Saul-Mirone/milkdown/issues) and [discussions](https://github.com/Saul-Mirone/milkdown/discussions) and couldn’t find anything (or linked relevant results below)

### Affected packages and versions

7.15.1

### Link to runnable example

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

Open the editor in an Android browser

Create any list type (unordered/ordered/todo)

Observe the behavior when pressing Enter for new items


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9e0e0188-2da3-40bb-9d9d-3e5f296ba66d

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/460ffa79-31b8-41e3-9dd8-16cd4335afc5

### Expected behavior

Normal single-spacing between list items

Automatic numbering increment in ordered lists

Consistent behavior with desktop browsers

### Actual behavior

Unordered lists and Todo lists:

Each line break automatically adds an extra blank line

Creates excessive vertical spacing between list items

Ordered lists:

Line breaks do not increment the numbering sequence

Other (please specify in steps to reproduce)

Android 13, XiaoMi 10s

Other (please specify in steps to reproduce)

diff --git a/packages/prose/src/toolkit/input-rules/custom-input-rules.ts b/packages/prose/src/toolkit/input-rules/custom-input-rules.ts
index 29c90927f10..adde7e884b0 100644
--- a/packages/prose/src/toolkit/input-rules/custom-input-rules.ts
+++ b/packages/prose/src/toolkit/input-rules/custom-input-rules.ts
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@ import type { EditorState, TextSelection, Transaction } from '../../state'
 import type { EditorView } from '../../view'
 
 import { Plugin, PluginKey } from '../../state'
+import { android, chrome } from '../browser'
 
 function run(
   view: EditorView,
@@ -76,6 +77,28 @@ export function customInputRules({ rules }: { rules: InputRule[] }): Plugin {
           })
           return false
         },
+        keydown: (view, event) => {
+          // On Chrome Android, prosemirror-view suppresses Enter keydown events
+          // to avoid input corruption during composition. It then relies on DOM
+          // mutation detection to retroactively handle Enter. However, this
+          // fallback fails with custom node views (e.g. list-item-block) whose
+          // wrapper DOM structure prevents the Enter detection heuristics from
+          // recognizing the mutation. We intercept Enter here — before
+          // prosemirror-view's suppression — and manually route it through
+          // handleKeyDown so that keymaps (splitListItem, etc.) work correctly.
+          if (!(android && chrome && (event as KeyboardEvent).key === 'Enter'))
+            return false
+          if (view.composing) return false
+          if (
+            view.someProp('handleKeyDown', (f) =>
+              f(view, event as KeyboardEvent)
+            )
+          ) {
+            event.preventDefault()
+            return true
+          }
+          return false
+        },
       },
       handleKeyDown(view, event) {
         if (event.key !== 'Enter') return false

issue_body: ### DIM Version Version 8.95.0 (release), 10/20/2025, 04:47:04 Chrome 141.0.0.0 ### Browser Details Chrome 141.0.7390.108 ### OS Details Windows 11 ### Describe the bug The Compare function does not work with Bushido armor set items when the DIM language is set to German. The German Bushido Set is labeled 'Weg des Krieger'. The 'Bushido Grips' for example are called ' "Weg des Kriegers"-Greifer'. Note the double quotation marks in the label. When clicking on compare, the "name:" filter does not show the correct amount of items of the specific selected armor item. When selecting this filter, instead of only showing the specific Bushido armor items, all items are shown. This error does not occur in other languages, e.g. English. How to reproduce: 1. Switch DIM to German language 2. Click Bushido/"Weg des Kriegers" armor item 3. Select "Vergleiche" from popup menu 4. Select filter, e.g. name:"Weg des Kriegers"-Greifer ### Logs ```shell ```

issue_title: Light.gg link for item logs user out of light.gg
issue_body:
### DIM Version

Version 8.85.0.4211 (beta), built on 8/14/2025, 5:29:49 PM

### Browser Details

Firefox

### OS Details

Windows 10

### Describe the bug

This is either a light.gg internal bug, or a DIM URL construction problem, or an integration problem from both.

When viewing an item's "large card", the external link to light.gg (adjacent to d2foundry.gg, etc.) forcibly logs the user out of light.gg and takes them to its homepage.  This was observed with the new weapon `Returned Memory`. 

| URL | Bugged | Notes |
| :-- | -- | :-- |
| `https://www.light.gg/db/en/items/4049127142?p=1844523823,106909392,3017780555,3673922083,1673863459,4105787911,`  | Yes | Generated by DIM, trailing comma.|
| `https://www.light.gg/db/en/items/4049127142`  | No | No URL params |
| `https://www.light.gg/db/en/items/4049127142?p=1844523823,106909392,3017780555,3673922083,1673863459,4105787911,`  | No | Removed trailing comma |
| `https://www.light.gg/db/en/items/4049127142?p=1844523823,106909392,3017780555,3673922083,1673863459,4105787911,0`  | No | Added zero at end |

Tinkering with light.gg, I suspect that after the trailing comma, the weapon's currently-slotted mod is expected, and for some reason the empty slot is becoming `""` instead of `"0"`.
pr_title: Fix light.gg URL generation to prevent trailing commas causing user logout

issue_title: Unable to un-save a saved search
issue_body:
### DIM Version

Version 8.81.1.4031 (beta), built on 7/16/2025, 12:16:41 PM

### Browser Details

Firefox 140.0.4 (64-bit)

### OS Details

Microsoft Windows 11 Home 10.0.26100 Build 26100

### Describe the bug

In the search bar on the Inventory page, DIM shows that I have a saved search for `is:exotic -is:masterwork upgrade`. If selected, the star on the search bar is not filled in, so it cannot be un-saved.

When going to the Search History page via Settings, I can see the same saved search there. If I use the search, the # Used count for the saved search does not increment, but the # Used for a similar search, `is:exotic and -is:masterwork and upgrade` does increment.

On the Search History page, if I click the filled-in star on `is:exotic -is:masterwork upgrade`, nothing happens. However, if I click the empty star on `is:exotic and -is:masterwork and upgrade` it fills in, saving the search. If I then click the filled-in star next to `is:exotic -is:masterwork upgrade`, it un-saves `is:exotic and -is:masterwork and upgrade`.

I'm guessing there's some bug around parsing the saved search and then save/unsave is acting on the post-parsing version of the search, making the pre-parsed version unable to be removed.

### Logs

```shell

```
pr_title: Look harder for matching queries when unsaving
