issue_title: Limit configuration available publicly in `config.json`
issue_body:
## Describe the bug

As [reported on dspace-tech](https://groups.google.com/g/dspace-tech/c/uuaEONDXytM), the `config.json` public availability could be flagged as an "information disclosure" vulnerability by security scanners.  This was also discussed in [today's Developers Meeting](https://wiki.lyrasis.org/display/DSPACE/2026-01-22+DSpace+Developers+Meeting).

This `config.json` is used by the DSpace UI for runtime configuration of the user interface.  The purpose of this configuration is to tell the DSpace UI which features are enabled on specific pages. The `config.json` should not include secure or private information.

The `config.json` is initially loaded via SSR (Server Side Rendering) which does not require it to be publicly available.  However, the `config.json` **must be publicly available** in CSR (Client Side Rendering) mode, unless the application state has been transferred from SSR mode (see [code in `main.browser.ts`](https://github.com/DSpace/dspace-angular/blob/main/src/main.browser.ts#L31-L44)). 

This means that, if you block access to the `config.json`, then CSR mode may not function properly in all situations.  This is especially true if your initial access to the site is a page that doesn't undergo SSR (e.g. search pages or similar).  **If you block access to the `config.json`, then any paths/pages included in [your `ssr > excludePathPatterns` settings](https://wiki.lyrasis.org/display/DSDOC9x/User+Interface+Configuration#UserInterfaceConfiguration-ServerSideRendering(SSR)Settings) will not function properly**.

Overall, this means that we should **limit the configurations available in the `config.json`** to only those configurations which are required by CSR or pose no "information disclosure" threat.

Possible configurations to move out of the `config.json` include:
* Any configuration that is specific to SSR (e.g. `rest.ssrBaseUrl` and the `serverSide` cache settings)\
* Potentially configurations that are better off moved to the backend.

Settings that may need to remain in the `config.json` include:
* `ui` settings - These tell Node.js where to start the UI.  However, they should always reference blocked/private ports in production scenarios because the UI is run behind a proxy in production mode.
* Other `rest` settings - These tell the UI where the public REST API is. So they always point to a public, findable server.
* Other settings used by the UI to change behavior (includes things like configuring results per page, languages, etc.). All of these are discoverable from the UI anyways.

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. See that the `config.json` is publicly available on the demo site: https://demo.dspace.org/assets/config.json

## Expected behavior
The `config.json` likely needs to remain public.  But, we can do a better job of limiting the configs available in that file to only those configs *required by CSR mode*.   Any SSR specific configs should be moved to a private configuration file which i
pr_title: Remove SSR related configuration from client app

issue_title: Typo in browse-by-taxonomy.component.ts
issue_body:
## Describe the bug
Typo on line 157 in `browse-by-taxonomy.component.ts` assigns faulty translation string to `this.description`. Affects `8.x` only. This was fixed for `9.x` onwards [in this commit addressing typos](https://github.com/DSpace/dspace-angular/commit/bfd61b2f19ceb4f078bead11b5daed9bd22acba3).

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Run DSpace frontend and backend 
2. Navigate to "Browsing By Subject Category" `/browse/srsc` on frontend
3. Observe Description (variable name, not translated string)

## Expected behavior
Fix the typo and have the translated string from a `.json5` file appear instead of a variable name.

## Related Work

PR #5305

issue_title: Items in Hong Kong/Macau are wrongly getting Chinese phone/fax hint text
issue_body:
### URL

N/A

### How to reproduce the issue?

1. Find any feature inside Hong Kong/Macau that does not have its `phone`/`'fax`/(or related fields) filled
2. Using the editor, add a e.g. Telephone field for editing
3. See "+86" in the hint text

### Screenshot(s) or anything else?

For example, the phone field has hint text "+86 [...]".

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/97176807-24cd-4fef-9c73-512a016b4314)

------

Hong Kong (currently officially Hong Kong Special Administrative Region, People's Republic of China, or "HKSAR, PRC") and Macau (currently officially Macau Special Administrative Region, People's Republic of China, or "MSAR, PRC") used to be separate political entities as colonies of the UK and Portugal respectively.

Both Hong Kong and Macau got their own country code when the relevant telecomms standards were first drafted:
- Hong Kong: +852
- Macau: +853

This is distinct from the "mainland Chinese" code +86 currently being used by People's Republic of China ("PRC", also commonly known simply as "China").

As part of the deals about decolonization and Chinese takeover (Hong Kong 1997; Macau 1999), it was agreed these country codes would be kept in use, and as of now, there are no plans to migrate the phone numbers to use the +86 prefix. The +852 and +853 phone numbers are currently still in use, as have been pre-takeover. (Note: we should not consider the technically possible case of "what happens in 2047/2049".)

Yet, the iD editor is completely unaware of this situation, and is showing the Chinese code +86 instead.

Those who know about this situation will correctly fill in +852/+853, but I am concerned those unaware would just blindly trust the editor and fill in +86, and then we will have invalid phone numbers documented in OSM.

Please review the relevant logic regarding the hint texts and cater for the on-the-ground situation of +852/+853 in both Hong Kong and Macau.

### Which deployed environments do you see the issue in?

Released version at openstreetmap.org/edit

### What version numbers does this issue effect?

2.32.0

### Which browsers are you seeing this problem on?

Other (please specify)
pr_title: Enable territory-level phone hints
app_description: A friendly editor for OpenStreetMap

issue_title: Presets for motorway link, trunk link, primary link won’t show up as recently used presets in some regions
issue_body: ### URL (you can test it anywhere) ### How to reproduce the issue? 1. Select a way or draw a new one. 2. Select four different presets to fill the four “recently used” slots. * e.g. Motorway, Trunk Road, Primary Road, Secondary Road 3. One after another, select all five link road types from the “Major Roads…” preset group. After these steps, only the Secondary Link and Tertiary Link presets are shown in my recently used presets – Trunk Link and Primary Link occupy two “invisible” slots: ### Screenshot(s) or anything else? * The presets Motorway Link, Trunk Link, Primary Link don’t show up as recently used presets, but they still take up slots. * Notably, the issue doesn’t occur for me on the development version, but I was unable to find a similar issue report, so I don’t think it’s really fixed. * Could this be caused by some kind of `localStorage` corruption? ### Which deployed environments do you see the issue in? Released version at openstreetmap.org/edit ### What version numbers does this issue effect? 2.36.0 ### Which browsers are you seeing this problem on? Firefox

It should behave similar to this:

issue_body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I am using the [correct version](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap#bootstrap-compatibility) of React-Bootstrap for my version of Bootstrap
- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/issues?q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues

issue_title: Custom `Tab` layouts are not accessible
issue_body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I am using the [correct version](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap#bootstrap-compatibility) of React-Bootstrap for my version of Bootstrap
- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/issues?q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the bug

Custom `Tab` layouts no longer appear to be keyboard-accessible in v2. Tab elements are not focusable via tabbing, and the selected tab can no longer be changed using the arrow keys. This seems to be a regression from v1.x.

- [v2 example (broken)](https://react-bootstrap.netlify.app/components/tabs/#custom-tab-layout)
- [v1 example (working)](https://react-bootstrap-v4.netlify.app/components/tabs/#tabs-custom-layout)

### Expected behavior

I expect v2 to work the way v1 did:
- Individual tabs should be focusable using the tab key
- A focused, unselected tab can be selected by hitting the space or enter keys
- When the tablist is focused, the selected tab can be changed using the arrow keys

### To Reproduce

Navigate to the [v2 example](https://react-bootstrap.netlify.app/components/tabs/#custom-tab-layout) and try to access the tabs using a keyboard.

### Reproducible Example

[v2 example](https://react-bootstrap.netlify.app/components/tabs/#custom-tab-layout)

Chrome

2.5

5.2

_No response_

issue_title: Bug - Accordion does not properly support aria-controls
issue_body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I am using the [correct version](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap#bootstrap-compatibility) of React-Bootstrap for my version of Bootstrap
- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/issues?q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the bug

The `aria-controls` attribute helps to establish a relationship between a control (such as a button or a header) and the element it controls (such as a collapsible panel or a tab panel). Currently if `aria-controls` is passed to the `AccordionHeader` subcomponent the attribute is added to the `h2` and there is no way to move this attribute to the button. ([Accessible example](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/accordion/examples/accordion/))

### Expected behavior

The `AccordionHeader` subcomponent should support the `aria-controls` property and apply it to the `button` instead of the `h2` so that screen reader users can better understand the relationship between the  `AccordionHeader` and the `AccordionBody`

### To Reproduce

1. Given a `AccordionHeader` and `AccordionBody`
2. Add an `id` to the `AccordionBody` ` id='accordion-body'`
3. Add `aria-controls='accordion-body'` to the `AccordionHeader`
4. Render the component
5. Inspect the DOM to see that  `aria-controls='accordion-body'` is incorrectly applied to the `h2` element and not the `button`

### Reproducible Example

https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/react-bootstrap-5-forked-4j5y7d?file=%2Fsrc%2FApp.js%3A19%2C18&workspaceId=6459cc5f-9c12-4ceb-9ca3-6d586cbadae4

### Screenshots

_No response_

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome, Firefox

### What version of React-Bootstrap are you using?

2.7.2

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.0

### Additional context

_No response_

issue_title: Dropdown.Toggle does not work correctly when using mousedown as rootCloseEvent
issue_body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I am using the [correct version](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap#bootstrap-compatibility) of React-Bootstrap for my version of Bootstrap
- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/issues?q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the bug

This was already reported back in 2018 (https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/issues/3196) and is still an issue.

Just create a dropdown with the `rootCloseEvent="mousedown"`. When tyring to close the dropdown with the toggle button the dropdown will flicker since it is hidden on mousedown but is being shown on onClick due to the logic in the toggle.

`mousedown` is in some circumstances essential to the functionality over the `click` rootCloseEvent.
For example I have a Drag&Drop Grabber to move the object where the dropdown lives. There will be no onClick event since I am dragging the item but I do want the dropdown to close when I drag the item.

```
<Dropdown>
    <Dropdown.Toggle variant="success" id="dropdown-basic">
        Dropdown Button
    </Dropdown.Toggle>

    <Dropdown.Menu rootCloseEvent="mousedown">
        <Dropdown.Item href="#/action-1">Action</Dropdown.Item>
        <Dropdown.Item href="#/action-2">Another action</Dropdown.Item>
        <Dropdown.Item href="#/action-3">Something else</Dropdown.Item>
    </Dropdown.Menu>
</Dropdown>
```

### Expected behavior

The toggle should support any valid `rootCloseEvent` as you would expect or the `mousedown` rootCloseEvent should be removed since its only supported for hiding and not showing.

### To Reproduce

_No response_

### Reproducible Example

https://codesandbox.io/s/xenodochial-frost-69cl6x

### Screenshots

_No response_

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

_No response_

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

_No response_

### What version of React-Bootstrap are you using?

2.9.0

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

5.3.2

### Additional context

_No response_
pr_title: fix: Prevent Dropdown Flickering on 'mousedown' rootCloseEvent

issue_title: Modal Aria Description applied to the wrong element
issue_body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I am using the [correct version](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap#bootstrap-compatibility) of React-Bootstrap for my version of Bootstrap
- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/issues?q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the bug

The modal component produces a dialog where the aria-describedby -attribute gets applied to a wrong element. 

### Expected behavior

aria-describedby -attribute is applied to the dialog at the same level with aria-labelledby

### To Reproduce

1. Go to vertically centered modal (BS4) https://react-bootstrap-v4.netlify.app/components/modal/#modal-vertically-centered
2. Add attribute to Modal-element source: aria-describedby="contained-modal-content-vcenter"
3. Add corresponding id to Modal.Body: <Modal.Body id="contained-modal-content-vcenter">
4. Launch the modal
5. Inspect the HTML-source and notice that aria-describedby is set at different level compared to aria-labelledby

### Reproducible Example

1. Go to vertically centered modal (BS4) https://react-bootstrap-v4.netlify.app/components/modal/#modal-vertically-centered
2. Add attribute to Modal-element source: aria-describedby="contained-modal-content-vcenter"
3. Add corresponding id to Modal.Body: <Modal.Body id="contained-modal-content-vcenter">
4. Launch the modal
5. Inspect the HTML-source and notice that aria-describedby is set at different level compared to aria-labelledby

### Screenshots

_No response_

### What operating system(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Windows, macOS, Linux

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome
Firefox

### What version of React-Bootstrap are you using?

1.6.5

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

4.6.1

### Additional context

With the latest version this works as expected (can be similarly reproduced at https://react-bootstrap.netlify.app/components/modal/#modal-vertically-centered) but 1.X.X should be still maintained

issue_title: Accessibility: Focusable elements outside of the modal
issue_body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I am using the [correct version](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap#bootstrap-compatibility) of React-Bootstrap for my version of Bootstrap
- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/issues?q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/react-bootstrap/react-bootstrap/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the bug

While the React Bootstrap modal is open, moving around with the keyboard using Tab, focuses on elements outside of the modal. Pressing TAB while navigating the modal should not result in the focus being put on element outside of the modal.

### Expected behavior

A user that use the keyboard to navigate a website and open a modal, should get his focus trapped within that modal until he press 'ESC' or the close button. 

### To Reproduce

1. Navigate to a button opening a modal with TAB
2. Hit enter

issue_title: [bug] Branch and window dragging use the wrong references
issue_body:
#### Describe the bug

This has happened in two cases: 
1) When a user creates a new branch and then tries to drag the webframe topbar to arrange it, it acts as if the user is dragging the original branch

2) When the user has one window selected and tries to drag another adjacent window, it pretends like the first window is the one still in focused. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8458a147-dbfb-4707-ad44-1241ac375e83)

The expected behavior would be that the user can click on any webframe and it would select and drag that topframe. Right now it seems to keep the first frame selected and because the mouse hovers over a topbar area it enables the ability to drag / move the frame.

issue_title: [bug] Message counting is potentially incorrect
issue_body:
#### Describe the bug
Usage count has been messed up potentially by this PR. https://github.com/onlook-dev/onlook/pull/2718

Note that the usage should be counted once per users request and also be reverted if an error occurs. This used to be the case but seems to have changed now
pr_title: Fix message usage counting to handle all error scenarios correctly
app_description: Onlook Monorepo

issue_title: Plus sign is incorrect in Last.fm URLs
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
If a song has the plus symbol (+) in it and you open the song's Last.fm page through the "View track page" button, the link will have the plus represented as %2B, when it should be %252B. This can lead to error pages ("Page Not Found").

**How to reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to for example https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1t4-DG_zf3c or https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rIPOOoQxquE and start scrobbling the song
2. Click on the song name in the extension to open it in Last.fm
3. The opened page will be an error or a version of the song with very few scrobbles, because the link is incorrect

**Screenshots**
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/978bc32a-796c-435b-9825-6664b8306916)

**Environment**
-   OS: Windows
-   Browser: Firefox 135.0.1
-   Extension version: 3.10.0
pr_title: Fix plus signs not working in links
app_description: Scrobble music all around the web!

issue_title: YouTube: “In this video” detected as track title, causing different videos to merge
issue_body: **Describe the bug** When scrobbling certain YouTube videos, Web Scrobbler detects “In this video” as the track title instead of the actual video title shown in the browser tab and on the YouTube page. As a result, different videos are treated as the same track internally. Any manual edits (title or album) applied to one video also affect the other. I also tried editing the JSON configuration and adding the specific video URLs, but this did not resolve the issue. This is happening me with this three specific videos: 1. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xBxwKeVGFeg 2. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aGSmxr-dUq0 3. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ukdidk1CkaQ **Expected behavior** The extension should use the actual video title instead of the “In this video” section, so each video can be scrobbled independently. **Screenshots** **Environment (please complete the following information)** - OS: Windows 11 - Browser: Firefox - Extension version: 3.18.0 **Additional context** My YouTube language is set to Spanish, so the detected text is “En este vídeo”.
pr_title: fix "In this video" for youtube.ts
app_description: Scrobble music all around the web!

issue_title: Scrobbler on Spotify detects the song, but does not save/upload when set to remaining time (-XX:XX) instead of song duration
issue_body: **Describe the bug** Playing a song on Spotify is properly detected by the extension, but does not save/upload the scrobble. **How to reproduce** Play a song on Spotify while the playbar is set to (`-XX:XX`) remaining time instead of song duration. **Expected behavior** Once it reaches the song duration percentage in the settings or 4 minutes, the song should be scrobbled. **Screenshots** **Debug logs** https://pastebin.com/HfGEmYgf **Environment (please complete the following information)** - OS: Windows 11 - Browser: Firefox 144.0 - Extension version: 3.17.0 **Additional context** Checking the extension's settings in Cache, the last successful Spotify scrobble as of writing was 3 weeks ago. Trying to scrobble with YouTube Music is fine. Changing the `Scrobble at X% duration` setting to its lowest (10%) does nothing. I assume that the extension/connector can't see the duration of the song.

issue_title: [BUG] Missing Focus Rings on Interactive Elements - Keyboard Navigation Issue
issue_body:
### Describe the bug.

*Problem*
Multiple interactive components throughout the website use `focus:outline-none` without providing alternative focus indicators, making it impossible for keyboard navigation users to see which element currently has focus.

*Affected Files & Lines*
1. `components/navigation/NavBar.tsx` (Lines 161, 169, 221)
2. `components/layout/DocsLayout.tsx` (Line 133)
3. `components/navigation/NavItem.tsx` (Line 58)

*Current Behavior*
Interactive elements (navigation items) have `focus:outline-none` applied without any alternative focus indicator, resulting in:
- No visible feedback when elements receive keyboard focus
- Impossible to determine current focus position when using Tab navigation
- Fails WCAG 2.1 accessibility guidelines

*Proposed Solution*
Replace instances of `focus:outline-none` with accessible focus ring styles


### Expected behavior
All interactive elements should have a clear, visible focus indicator when navigated via keyboard.

### Screenshots
*Video Recordings*

*before*
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e687199a-fe27-4654-aad3-611e3eb507e1

*after*
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/66da3a41-95ad-485b-baa8-fe00a448dc40


### How to Reproduce
1. Open the AsyncAPI website locally
2. Press `Tab` key repeatedly to navigate through the page
3. Observe the navigation bar,
4. Notice that no visible focus indicator appears on focused elements


### 🖥️ Device Information [optional]

- Operating System (OS):
- Browser:
- Browser Version:


### 👀 Have you checked for similar open issues?

- [x] I checked and didn't find similar issue

### 🏢 Have you read the Contributing Guidelines?

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/asyncapi/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Are you willing to work on this issue ?

Yes I am willing to submit a PR!
pr_title: fix: add visible focus rings for keyboard navigation

issue_title: [BUG] Contribution Guide button on Community Dashboard redirects to incorrect page
issue_body: ### Describe the bug. On the Community → Dashboard page, the “Contribution Guide” button redirects to an incorrect page instead of the expected AsyncAPI contribution guidelines. Other contribution-related links (e.g. “View on GitHub”) work correctly, but this specific button does not point to the intended documentation, which may confuse new contributors trying to get started. From a UX perspective, this breaks consistency and the user’s mental model of navigation. ### Expected behavior Clicking the Contribution Guide button should redirect users to the official AsyncAPI contribution guidelines, for example: - asyncapi/community/CONTRIBUTING.md or the equivalent rendered documentation page ### Screenshots Community Dashboard showing the Contribution Guide button Page where the button currently redirects Expected destination (CONTRIBUTING.md) which other links already use ### How to Reproduce 1. Go to AsyncAPI website 2. Navigate to Community → Dashboard 3. Click on the Contribution Guide button 4. issue_title: [BUG] Next.js attempts to prefetch rss.xml as a page, resulting in 404
issue_body: ### Describe the bug. While loading the blog page, Next.js attempts to prefetch `/rss.xml` as if it were a Next.js page, resulting in a 404 request for a non-existent data route. This happens because Next.js automatically prefetches links rendered with ``, and treats `rss.xml` as a page instead of a static file. **Observed request:** GET /_next/data//rss.xml.json?lang=rss.xml 404 ### Expected behavior - Static resources such as `rss.xml` should not be prefetched as Next.js pages. - No 404 requests should be triggered during route prefetching. **Actual behavior** - Next.js attempts to prefetch `rss.xml` and requests a corresponding `_next/data` JSON route which does not exist. - A 404 error appears in the browser console/network logs. ### Screenshots ### How to Reproduce 1. Open https://www.asyncapi.com/blog 2. Open browser Developer Tools 3. Navigate to the **Console** tab 4. Observe a 404 request for: /_next/data//rss.xml.json?lang=rss.xml **Notes** - This does not affect user functionality. - The RSS feed itself works as expected. - This appears to be a Next.js prefetch optimization issue. ### 🖥️ Device Information [optional] - Browser: Google Chrome - Browser Version: Latest - OS: Windows 11 - Website version: Production (asyncapi.com) ### 👀 Have you checked for similar open issues? - [x] I checked and didn't find similar issue ### 🏢 Have you read the Contributing Guidelines? - [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/asyncapi/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Are you willing to work on this issue ? Yes I am willing to submit a PR!

issue_title: [BUG] Memory leak in ScrollButton due to missing scroll event listener cleanup
issue_body: ### Describe the bug. The ScrollButton component adds a scroll event listener using useEffect, but the listener is never removed. Because the component is mounted globally in _app.tsx, the event listener stays attached for the lifetime of the app. The handler is also defined as an anonymous function, which prevents it from being removed later. This can lead to a memory leak and unnecessary state updates during scrolling. ### Expected behavior The ScrollButton component should register a scroll event listener when it mounts and properly remove the listener when it unmounts, preventing memory leaks and unnecessary state updates. ### Screenshots ### How to Reproduce 1. Open the AsyncAPI website at https://www.asyncapi.com/ 2. Scroll down the page until the “Back to Top” button appears 3. Open the source code for components/buttons/ScrollButton.tsx 4. Observe that a scroll event listener is added inside a useEffect hook 5. Note that the effect does not return a cleanup function to remove the event listener ### 🖥️ Device Information [optional] - Operating System (OS): macOS - Browser: Google Chrome - Browser Version: 143.0.7499.170 ### 👀 Have you checked for similar open issues? - [x] I checked and didn't find similar issue ### 🏢 Have you read the Contributing Guidelines? - [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/asyncapi/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Are you willing to work on this issue ? Yes I am willing to submit a PR!
pr_title: fix: clean up scroll event listener in ScrollButton to prevent memory leak

issue_title: [BUG] Missing image asset canela-quetzalli.webp returns 404 on blog page
issue_body: ### Describe the bug. While browsing the blog page, the site attempts to load an image asset `canela-quetzalli.webp`, which returns a 404 error. This results in a broken image request and unnecessary network errors. ### Expected behavior The referenced image should exist and load successfully, or the reference should be updated/removed if the asset is no longer used. ### Screenshots Browser console shows: ### How to Reproduce 1. Open https://www.asyncapi.com/ in a browser. 2. Open **Developer Tools** and navigate to the **Console** tab. 3. Refresh the page. 4. Refresh the page a second time (hard refresh) 5. Observe a failed request for: https://www.asyncapi.com/canela-quetzalli.webp (404) ### Notes - The error may not appear on the first load due to Next.js hydration and storage initialization. - The issue reproduces reliably after a second reload. - No network request failure is required to trigger the error. ### 🖥️ Device Information [optional] - **Browser:** Chrome - **Browser Version:** Latest - **Operating System:** Windows 11 - **Node.js:** 20.x (local testing) - **Website Version:** Production (asyncapi.com) ### 👀 Have you checked for similar open issues? - [x] I checked and didn't find similar issue ### 🏢 Have you read the Contributing Guidelines? - [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/asyncapi/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Are you willing to work on this issue ? issue_title: Unable to re enable legends on line charts when there are multiple series
issue_body: ### Description ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Using ApexChart v5.10.4 on an Angular / Ionic Project 2. Visit the link below for a demo available on ApexChart Website https://apexcharts.com/angular-chart-demos/line-charts/dashed/ ### Expected Behavior A visibility of a series can be turned on and off by clicking on the legend. The legend text of a series will be grayed out to indicate that a chart series is hidden, and when clicked again on the legend item, it should appear again in (defined) colour to indicate that the chart series is enabled /visible again. ### Actual Behavior 1. When clicked on a legend item first time it correctly hides the series and grays out the legend item on the chart. 2. When clicked again on the legend item, the series appears on the chart, but the legend item stays disabled (Grayed out) 3. When clicked again on the disabled legend item, nothing happens after this point 4. When clicked on a 2nd series legend on the same chart, depending on the status of the legend 1 item, the enable / disable happens in a weird way. 5. See screenshots below for step 0 - 3. ### Screenshots ### Reproduction Link Don't think another demo is required as the issue is clearly visible on ApexChart line chart documentation https://apexcharts.com/angular-chart-demos/line-charts/dashed/
pr_title: fix: legend stays grayed out after re-enabling hidden series (#5189)

issue_title: Inconsistent tooltip title formatting for multiple-y-series tooltip configuration when only value formatter is provided
issue_body:
### Description
There is an inconsistency in the tooltip label behavior with tooltip that targets multiple y series when a formatter function is provided only for the tooltip value. The default formatter applied to the tooltip title differs in this case compared to when no formatter is provided at all.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure a tooltip with a custom formatter to multiple y series for the value only.
2. Observe the default formatting applied to the tooltip title.
3. Compare this with the tooltip behavior when no formatter is provided.

### Expected Behavior
The default formatter for the tooltip title should remain consistent regardless of whether a value formatter is supplied.

### Actual Behavior
The default formatter applied to the tooltip title changes when a value formatter is used without a title formatter.

### Screenshots

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e301b22-8e2e-47c2-8623-f863c3e4b82f)


### Reproduction Link
https://codepen.io/rahulr-96/pen/dyxrQrL

@@ -328,12 +328,15 @@ export default class BarDataLabels {

-      let xDivision = w.globals.gridWidth / w.globals.dataPoints
+      let xDivision = dataPointsDividedWidth
 
       totalDataLabelsX =

-        (w.globals.isXNumeric ? barWidth : xDivision) +
+        (w.globals.isXNumeric

issue_title: Line Plot Y-range incorrect when series has multiple points with same x-value
issue_body:
### Description

When plotting a line chart, the y-axis range is not correct if the series has multiple points with the same x value and one of those points is outside of the range of all other points in the series. 

From my testing, it appears that this occurs when there are more than two points that share the same x value. Only the first two points are used to calculate the range. All others seem to be discarded.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a line plot with at-least three points that have the same x value. Ensure the last point has a y value that is greater than any other y value in the series.

### Expected Behavior
Y-axis range for axis is calculated from absolute range of series

### Actual Behavior
Y-axis range axis range is not calculated correctly when multiple points in a series have the same x value.

### Screenshots

![image](https://github.com/apexcharts/apexcharts.js/assets/37275661/abf83c24-f84e-4ff7-82f4-f7b0ed7c6ff0)

### Reproduction Link

https://codepen.io/Alan-Kalbfleisch/pen/KKLorJe
pr_title: Fix for issue 4560
app_description: A JavaScript Chart Library

issue_title: Y-axis ranges assigned to incorrect axes when using xy zoom selection
issue_body:
### Description

When plotting multiple y-axes each with multiple series assigned, using xy zoom selection results in the range for all y-axes other than the first in the list of the y-axes being incorrect.


### Steps to Reproduce

See details in reproduction link section.

### Expected Behavior
When zooming with xy selection, visible y-axes min and max ranges are set using the user's selection.

### Actual Behavior
When zooming with xy selection axis ranges are assigned in order of the yaxes list regardless of whether they are visible or not.

### Screenshots


https://github.com/apexcharts/apexcharts.js/assets/37275661/3ca46a39-78cc-4c23-b003-835b082acbfe



### Reproduction Link

https://codepen.io/Alan-Kalbfleisch/pen/WNqbQRg

Using the chart in the link above, open the console and make a zoom selection. In the console, note the ranges that have been attributed to each axis. Axes with ids 0 and 1 are for Series 1 data. Axes with ids 2 and 3 are for Series 2 data. The range for the second visible axis is assigned to the second axis (id 1) instead of the correct axis (id 2). This results in the range of any y axes other than the first visible axes to be incorrect.

issue_title: Inconsistent/buggy yaxis ticks (too many or none)
issue_body:
### Description

yaxis ticks have had some significant changes in version 3.46 and 3.47, and the behavior of the newer versions is worse IMO.

In version 3.45, for my data sets and chart size of 250px height, I was consistently seeing about 5 yaxis ticks.
In version 3.46, with the same data and height, I was suddenly seeing double the ticks. 10 ticks is too many for a 250px height chart, and looks significantly worse IMO.
In version 3.47+, I'm still seeing about 10 ticks, but have noticed that with some data sets, there are zero ticks when there definitely should be some.

I know I can set the number of number of yaxis ticks with the `tickAmount` option, but it would be nice if the default behavior was sufficient. Also, for some strange reason, starting in 3.47.0, if I set the tickAmount, it can result in the first yaxis tick being negative, even though all the values are positive. Again, I'd like the min and max to be automatic/dynamic, not hardcoded to zero.

This could be 3 separate issues, but I think they could all be related to the same function(s).

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a few simple line charts with any data you choose. My series values are typically between 5 and 10,000.
2. Compare the yaxis behavior between apexcharts versions 3.45.2, 3.46.0, 3.47.0, and latest.

### Expected Behavior

The number and spacing of yaxis ticks should not clutter the chart, and should be somewhat consistent over time.

### Actual Behavior

The number and spacing of yaxis ticks has changed significantly in versions 3.46 and 3.47, and the ticks have cluttered the chart in these later versions. Sometimes the ticks are missing altogether.

### Screenshots

This matrix demonstrates how things have changed over time, and the bug in 3.47+ where there are no ticks:

![Apex Chart Version Comparisons](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2ebc57fc-fc42-4188-8cf9-e11fd2983b62)

### Reproduction Link

Chart with missing ticks: https://codepen.io/jsmartt/pen/OJeOLwr

Comparisons between version 3.45.2 and latest with the same data: https://codepen.io/jsmartt/pen/poXdzxJ & https://codepen.io/jsmartt/pen/wvLPvrK

Chart with yaxis min set to negative number, even though all values are positive: https://codepen.io/jsmartt/pen/mdZqdqZ (broke in 3.47.0)

issue_title: Incorrect tick alignment in heatmap using irregular timeseries
issue_body:
### Description

When using a heatmap and having inconsistent timeseries (e.g. one time series is missing some ticks) the data is not visualized correctly. If there are missing values on the x axis no gap is shown instead the next data point is directly placed next to the previous data point. Hovering the item shows the correct datetime of the x value, but the visualization is broken. Here is a [codepen ](https://codepen.io/Schweini/pen/poMYEgL) that shows the behaviour (line vs heatmap) based on datetimes as x axis. Series 1 should start at 15:30 and not be visualized at 10:30. Next to that also gaps within a time range should be handled properly.

A line chart is able to handle that as documented here: https://apexcharts.com/javascript-chart-demos/area-charts/irregular-timeseries
Maybe this is related to this historic issue: [3483](https://github.com/apexcharts/apexcharts.js/issues/3483) (PR: [3505](https://github.com/apexcharts/apexcharts.js/pull/3505))

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a heatmap based on datetime as x axis
2. Create multiple series that contain an inconsistent length of data

### Expected Behavior

Gaps inside the series is visualized as white space and x values are correctly aligned across the x axis. Example in elastic:
![grafik](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8d07f27e-ba0f-4f4c-95f5-267efc4fe342)

### Actual Behavior

![grafik](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19711873-0826-432c-b336-e487f27656a5)
Line chart is working as expected, heatmap not.

### Reproduction Link

https://codepen.io/Schweini/pen/poMYEgL
pr_title: Fix heatmap gaps and ticket alignment

issue_title: Bug in clipping behaviour of right-most x-Axis annotation
issue_body:
### Description

In the provided example, we have an area chart with multiple data series and annotations on y- and x-Axis.
One of the x-Axis annotations should be placed at the last x-value of the chart.

Unfortunately, this annotation will not be present at all screensizes even if the chart has enough space to display it.
After doing some research, in the apexcharts code, the problem seems to be the following:

In the file `XAxisAnnotations.js` the function `addXaxisAnnotation` is reponsible for placing the annotations in the chart.
The placement itself is bound to a condition which is `clipX1` in the provided example. This condition is being evaluated in `src/modules/annotations/Helpers.js` by the function `getX1X2`:

In [this if condition](https://github.com/apexcharts/apexcharts.js/blob/main/src/modules/annotations/Helpers.js#L222), the value of `clipped` is being set to `true` while the bug described below occurs. When console logging `xP` and `w.globals.gridWidth` here, you will see that the only difference between these two numbers is at the 13. decimal place caused by **JavaScript not being able to process large floats precisely**.

{ xP: 883.733333587646**6**, globals: 883.733333587646**5** }

After adding `.toFixed(10)` to both of the numbers inside the condition, the bug seems to be fixed.
Since in our use-case we are required to always show the third annotation in our UI, we would be very happy if the calculation of these numbers could become more reliable.

Also, I found this issue which might be related to the cause/fix: [issue #4734](https://github.com/apexcharts/apexcharts.js/issues/4734)

### Steps to Reproduce

The reproduction link is an area chart containing multiple series and a total of **3 x-Axis annotations**.

1. Open the playground linked below
2. Depending on your screen size, you might or might not have the third annotation missing
3. Use the resize function in the middle between the code section and the UI to slowly resize the chart
4. After waiting for the chart to re-render and adapt to the new screensize, check if the third annotation at x=2075 has been clipped or unclipped
5. If the visibility of the x=2075 annotation has not changed, go back to step 3 and repeat

### Expected Behavior

The last x-Axis annotation is always present if there is enough space.

### Actual Behavior

The last x-Axis annotation is only visible every ~2px and then invisible again for another ~3px.

### Screenshots

906px container width:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/276cb956-af3e-4b65-84cd-1a3ca20c0646)

905px container width:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6681610c-20f4-4e56-8689-0abaa16ca7f2)

### Reproduction Link

[Vue SFC Playground](https://play.vuejs.org/#eNrtWutO4zgUfpUoaAVI0KZ3YABtoUWa1d40zGp2tZkfJnFbD6mddRxoQbz7HjtJ4zhpmY7UXYlJkUJ9Lvbxd5zPTnqe7WEYNh5ibJ/Z55HHSSisCIs4vHQpmYeMC+vZYvQXFlOBfevFmnA2t/bBYf/dymIfhXjhzRAX0b5LXbqyPzg4tC4urWeXWtYD4hYLBWE0si4SkWW5tnJz7bNMImUTRsUNmpNgKRWu/QclD5hH
pr_title: fix annotation clipping calculation caused by unprecise floats

@@ -219,7 +219,7 @@ export default class Helpers {

issue_title: Sidebar: When 'sidebar.enabled' is false, get errors when trying to find in files
issue_body:
Following the discussion from #1555 :

One bug I did notice though, when `sidebar.enabled` is `false` (after #1555), and I try and 'find in files' with `Control+Shift+F`, I get an error:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13532591/36218374-f8ffe856-1168-11e8-93c7-d23761052d1d.png) 

What we might want to eventually do is _always_ create the sidebar + sidebar content pane, but if `sidebar.enabled` is `false`, we just hide them (or they start hidden, and we only `show` them if `sidebar.enabled` is true`). Then, if a user does `Control+Shift+B` to toggle the sidebar, or `Control+Shift+F` to find in files, we can make it visible.
pr_title: Allow Sidebar to be Disabled

issue_title: Signature help doesn't always disappear

👉  https://www.onivim.io/Insiders -->

Oni Version:
Neovim Version (Linux only):
Operating System:

#### Describe your issue
When the signature help tooltip appears it occasionally does not disappear once the input is complete. Calling the redraw function in oni seems to trigger it disappearing but scrolling does nothing nor does clicking.
#### Expected behaviour
Once I have input the argument to a function and leave insert mode the signature help tooltip should be dismissed.

The tool tip persists

In a `ts` or `js` import a function from a typed module, type out a function call e.g. `fs.readdir(|` once the cursor is in the parens the tool tip appears but *Occasionally* for no clear reason it persists till redraw is called

issue_title: Regression: File-explorer drag and drop
issue_body:
<!-- Want this issue prioritized? Please consider supporting the project and becoming an insider!
👉  https://www.onivim.io/Insiders -->
Dragging and dropping in the file explorer, doesn't appear to be working anymore.
I think this might be as a result of other dragging and dropping in oni. While working on #1406, I noted that there's a bit of a delicate balance between the dragging files onto oni and the file explorer drag and drop as higher level events can swallow the event transmission.

I think if `stopPropagation()` is being called on `document.body` then this will prevent events being transmitted down to the file explorer.

In the long run I think all drag and drop functionality needs to be specifically scoped to target elements and events on body should be avoided aka `querySelector(.active .editor).addEventListener("drop...)` as opposed to more general handlers
pr_title: Feature/add file drag and drop handler

issue_title: Oni segfaults on startup after upgrading to glibc 2.28
issue_body:
Hi!

Thanks for this amazing project :) 
If I can help by providing more information or anything, just let me know.

**Oni Version:** 0.3.6
**Neovim Version (Linux only):** 0.3.1
**Operating System:** Linux (Arch)

**Issue:**
After upgrading glibc to version 2.28, Oni segfaults on startup. Downgrading to glibc 2.27 works around this issue.

**Expected behavior:** Oni starts up.

**Actual behavior:** Segfault on startup

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Upgrade to glibc 2.28
2. Try to start Oni

**Edit:**
Seems like other projects face the same issue:
* Signal: https://github.com/signalapp/Signal-Desktop/issues/2610, https://github.com/signalapp/Signal-Desktop/issues/2600
* AUR package of Skype using patchelf to link to glibc 2.27: https://aur.archlinux.org/cgit/aur.git/commit/?h=skypeforlinux-preview-bin&id=e3fa08c20b44

issue_title: Slight snippet differences
issue_body:
<!-- Want this issue prioritized? Please consider supporting the project and becoming an insider!
👉  https://www.onivim.io/Insiders -->

I was testing some Python snippets before I recommended them to someone, and realised there is 2 small differences between ours and VSCode snippets, or at least it appears to be that way.

1. We require the `body` to be a list.
2. We don't support trailing commas.

https://github.com/Microsoft/vscode-python/blob/master/snippets/python.json is the example I was using. The last three snippets body is a single string rather than a string in a list. Also, the final snippet is terminated with `},`, which seems to both break the snippets and also stop the menu option appearing somehow? Removing that comma fixed it though.

issue_title: Markdown Preview Dim Persists
issue_body:
<!-- Love oni? Please consider supporting our collective:
👉  https://opencollective.com/oni/donate -->

@TalAmuyal Fantastic work on the markdown preview 🎉 

I've been using it a fair bit vs less elegant terminal vim plugins.
Whilst using it I noticed that after it opens it sets a `not-focused` class on the editor element which isnt removed by closing the plugin (by which I mean toggling it, not sure if that kills the process). I've found that by removing that class from the editor component the dimming goes away.

issue_title: Unable to remove background highlight on Note
issue_body: ## Step to reproduce 1. Create thought "Excepteur Sint Occaecat Cupidatat Non Proident" 2. Add a note "Ut Enim Ad Minim Veniam" 3. Tap Text Color button 4. Select Green Highlight Background button (this could be any color) 5. Verify that the Note's background is green and Note's font color is black 6. Tap Green Highlight Background button again ## Current behavior Green highlight background remains on the Note and the Note text font color turns to white. White Text Color and Green Background buttons are marked as active with white border. Selecting Green Text Color button will make the note invisible due to the same color applied on background and font. **Mobile** **Video:** https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d328b65-9d0a-48a7-b9d0-1aba42d4df45 ## Expected behavior Note background color should revert to default of grey on black background. Text color should only apply 1 each of the time; text and background colors should not be applied on the same time.

issue_title: [iOS] Faux caret mis-positioned when toggling table view
issue_body:
Tested on iOS Safari

## Steps to Reproduce

```
- A
  - B
    - C
```

1. Set the cursor on `B`. 
2. Set the caret at the end of `B`. 
3. Tap table view toolbar button.

## Current Behavior

The faux caret appears in the wrong place.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/db730586-6b8f-4e9f-b912-4a52a398126e

## Expected Behavior

The faux caret should appear in the same place where the real caret will appear.

---

It seems like this issue only occurs on load. If you perform another action such as creating and deleting a new thought, then the faux caret appears in the correct place after following the steps above.

Click Favorite button 4. Tap Hamburger button ## Current behavior A little scroller bar is visible next to the favorited thought with no text. This is reproducible on mobile but user needs to trigger the visibility by scrolling the area next to the favorited thought **Video on mobile:** https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/940b95ec-8f09-494f-8df2-a60c006ade41 ## Expected behavior Scroller bar should not be added onto the favorited thought with no text

issue_title: Drag-and-drop incorrectly activated after tapping uncle
issue_body:
Tested on desktop Chrome. 

## Steps to Reproduce

```
- A
  - B
- C
```

1. Set the cursor on `A`. 
2. Long press the bullet of `A` to activate multiselect. 
3. Click to cancel multiselect. 
4. Click the text of `C`. 
   - I clicked towards the bottom right.
   - The click location or the length of the click _may_ matter. 

## Current Behavior

Drag-and-drop is activated and stays active even when the mouse button is not held down. 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/792a4a71-ae41-4781-991b-df56af81db52

## Expected Behavior

Neither drag-and-drop nor long press should be activated on a simple click.

issue_title: Favorite context breadcrumb of bold, colored thought is rendered with ellipsis
issue_body:
## Steps to Reproduce

``` 
- A
  - B
    - C
``` 

1. Set the cursor on `C`. 
2. Add to favorites.
3. Cursor back (→). 
4. Set color. 
5. Bold. 
6. Open Sidebar. 

## Current Behavior

The context breadcrumb `B` has an ellipsis. 

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e160751c-bccb-4522-bb8d-c1ec15d36f39)

## Expected Behavior

There should be no ellipsis because the thought is not long enough to ellipsize.

issue_title: Command Center background not loaded in offline PWA
issue_body:
This is a variant of #3492 that isn't covered by the solution in #3550.

## Setup

This is reproducible when em is added to the home screen using Safari's Add to Home Screen feature. This PWA behaves very similarly to the Capacitor app when the device is offline, though I can't say if the caching policy is any different.

The simplest way to reproduce this is to navigate to [staging](https://em-proto--staging2-cxls71a0.web.app/) in mobile Safari and add it your home screen and follow the instructions below.

To reproduce on localhost is a bit trickier, as a PWA app served with the live reload dev server **cannot be added to the Home Screen in fullscreen mode** (it will merely open the app as a new tab in mobile Safari). However, there is a workaround. You can `yarn servebuild` to serve the production build, which **can** be added as a full screen app to the home screen. Once it's added, kill the `servebuild` process and start the dev server. Since the IP and port are the same, you should be able to now access your localhost dev server through the PWA that was previously added to your home screen. If you kill it and reopen it a second time then it will clear the cache and now serve the live reload server.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open em as PWA with the internet on.
2. Enter Airplane mode and turn off WiFi.
3. Kill em from the app switcher.
4. Open em as PWA while offline.

## Current Behavior

The [service worker](https://github.com/cybersemics/em/blob/main/src/serviceWorkerRegistration.ts) will serve the entire app from the cache while offline, but the Command Center background image is not cached. When the Command Center is opened, the background image is missing.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ff1940be-11c6-4c13-af78-463ab069eb11

## Expected Behavior

The Command Center background image should be cached and work while offline.
pr_title: fix: Preload Webp image for PWA

It should not create a new subthought.

index 005739943f6..13de19c1a18 100644
--- a/android/app/src/main/java/com/emtheapp/em/MainActivity.java
+++ b/android/app/src/main/java/com/emtheapp/em/MainActivity.java
@@ -1,5 +1,30 @@
 package com.emtheapp.em;
 
+import android.os.Bundle;
+import androidx.core.graphics.Insets;
+import androidx.core.view.ViewCompat;
+import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsCompat;
 import com.getcapacitor.BridgeActivity;
 
-public class MainActivity extends BridgeActivity {}
+public class MainActivity extends BridgeActivity {
+
+    @Override
+    protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
+        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState);
+
+        // When the keyboard (IME) opens, Android sends window insets to the FrameLayout that contains the WebView.
+        // This causes the WebView to resize, which causes the viewport to resize and breaks position: fixed elements.
+        // To fix this, we can strip the insets so the FrameLayout (and therefore the WebView) don't resize when the keyboard
+        // opens.
+        //
+        // This is the Android equivalent of Capacitor's iOS Keyboard { resize: 'none' }, but because that option doesn't
+        // exist on Android, we have to do it manually instead.
+        
+        ViewCompat.setOnApplyWindowInsetsListener(
+            findViewById(android.R.id.content),
+            (view, insets) -> new WindowInsetsCompat.Builder(insets)
+                .setInsets(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.ime(), Insets.NONE)
+                .build()
+        );
+    }
+}

issue_title: Slate selection doesn't update when dragging browser selection focus into `contentEditable={false}` element
issue_body:
## Description

When I drag my browser selection focus into a `contentEditable={false}` DOM element that's being rendered by a non-void Slate element renderer, Slate's selection doesn't get updated. At times, this causes non-highlighted text to be deleted when pressing <kbd>Backspace</kbd> (first recording below). At other times, this causes a <kbd>Backspace</kbd> press to crash the page (second recording below).

## Recording

![A demonstration of the bug in Slate's checklists example, in which pressing Backspace causes non-highlighted text to be deleted](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/886c9ea8-15c7-436e-9b8f-169e26ce2ff3)

![A demonstration of the bug in Slate's checklists example, in which pressing Backspace causes the page to crash](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/46a2285b-a832-45a7-9f1d-3457b4291784)

## Sandbox

https://www.slatejs.org/examples/check-lists

## Expectation

In the above recordings, that only the visually highlighted text would be deleted on <kbd>Backspace</kbd> and that the page would not crash.

## Environment

- Slate Version: `main` (confirmed locally with 7e77a93)
- Operating System: Windows 11
- Browser: Chrome, Firefox

## Context

I'm in the process of working on a fix, since this bug affects my project (we have a block type that renders a larger `contentEditable={false}` element above its text content, so it's a bit easier to trigger). I'll submit a PR once that's ready; just wanted to give y'all a heads-up here first.

issue_title: Inserting line break on `insertSoftBreak` breaks scrolling
issue_body:
**Description**
When inserting a soft break (line break) using `Shift+Enter` in Chrome (only), the editor incorrectly scrolls away from the cursor position. This issue occurs because the `scrollSelectionIntoView` function is called with a `DOMRange` that returns zero-dimension coordinates (`{x: 0, y: 0, width: 0, height: 0}`), causing the editor to scroll to the top-left corner of the page instead of keeping the cursor in view. The issue should be fixed in `DOMEditor.toDOMRange`

**Recording**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f3c756c7-aa0d-418c-8e49-db4f77bccf80

**Steps**

First, override `editor.insertSoftBreak` to

```ts
Transforms.insertText(editor, '\n')
```

To reproduce the behavior:

1. Open a Slate editor in Chrome
2. Navigate to the end of the document (scroll down if necessary)
3. Press `Shift+Enter` to insert a soft break
4. Observe that the editor scrolls away from the cursor position, often to an incorrect location

**Expectation**
The editor should maintain the cursor's position in the viewport after inserting a soft break.

**Environment**
- Slate Version: 0.117.0
- Operating System: macOS
- Browser: Chrome only

issue_title: Function `Transforms. insertFragment ` is not working properly when insert a fragment with two paragraphs
issue_body:
**Description**
When this function is called to insert a fragment with two paragraphs, the two paragraphs will be merged and inserted as inline nodes.

**Steps**
To reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to https://www.slatejs.org/examples/paste-html
2. Select the last two paragraphs and copy the content
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d186cdfb-b7a4-43f9-8cd5-23e150179b87)
3. Put the cursor in the **middle of one paragraph** (in this example it's before text`of doing exactly that`)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8b71138e-30a1-4e75-aad7-18d7ff337108)
4. paste from clipboard
5. Easy to notice the copied two paragraphs are merged into one and inserted in the cursor
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d3ee0382-7129-402f-9785-678606c894dc)


**Expectation**
It should create a new paragraph
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9d562f92-4fc3-4a06-9f22-fd8fd78da04b)

**Environment**
- Slate Version: 0.110.2
- Operating System: macOS
- Browser: Chrome
- TypeScript Version: N/A

**Context**
The root cause of this issue is the incorrect behavior of `Transforms. insertFragment` when the fragment is two paragraphs.

Here is a new test case which can reproduce this issue, please create it in the repo to reproduce.

`packages/slate/test/transforms/insertFragment/of-blocks/block-middle-two-blocks.tsx`
```typescript
/** @jsx jsx */
import { Transforms } from 'slate'
import { jsx } from '../../..'

export const run = (editor, options = {}) => {
  Transforms.insertFragment(
    editor,
    <fragment>
      <block>one</block>
      <block>two</block>
    </fragment>,
    options
  )
}
export const input = (
  <editor>
    <block>
      wo
      <cursor />
      rd
    </block>
  </editor>
)
export const output = (
  <editor>
    <block>woone</block>
    <block>
      two
      <cursor />
      rd
    </block>
  </editor>
)
```

issue_title: Undo doesn't work properly after drag & drop text
issue_body:
**Description**
Undo doesn't work properly after drag & drop text.

**Recording**

https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/assets/1988999/b058c02d-829a-4686-80b3-72d2afa22a71


**Steps**
To reproduce the behavior:
1. Open rich text example: https://www.slatejs.org/examples/richtext
2. Turn the quoted text into a heading element
3. Select part of text in the heading element, drag and drop it into the paragraph element
4. Undo

**Expectation**
Content of the heading element should be restored.

**Actual
Content of the heading element is restored.
And the heading element is turned back to quoted text.
It looks like that undo is invoked twice.

**Environment**
- Slate Version: 0.100.0
- Operating System: Windows 10
- Browser: Edge 119.0.2151.44

issue_title: Double-clicking word in Firefox selects adjacent nodes
issue_body:
**Description**
When using Firefox and double-clicking a word that has a mark (e.g., bold), the selection expands into the adjacent text nodes. This makes transforms like `wrapNodes` wrap multiple text nodes, instead of just the one with a mark.

This issue seems very similar to #5560

**Recording**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c099a3fb-d29d-4c90-866e-086d33fb83a4

**Sandbox**
https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/slate-reproductions-forked-gz34mg
(based on a minimal version of the inlines example)

**Steps**
To reproduce the behavior:
1. Open Firefox
1. Go to the sandbox above
1. Double-click on the word "ipsum"
1. Click the "Insert link" button
1. The link is now wrapped around the adjacent text nodes as well

**Expectation**
The link should only be wrapped around the text node that was selected when double-clicking.

**Environment**
- Slate Version: 0.118
- Operating System: macOS
- Browser: Firefox

issue_title: onValueChange not firing on MacOS "Add period with double-space" feature
issue_body:
**Description**
When triggering MacOS's "Add period with double-space" feature, `onSelectionChange` is fired rather than `onValueChanged`

**Recording**
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/524677f7-2617-4011-9beb-faa58b4c3e82)

**Sandbox**
https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/laughing-edison-h7kxvk

**Steps**
To reproduce the behavior:
1. Make sure "add period with double-space" is enabled (should be enabled by default)
  1.1 System Settings > Keyboard > Text Input > Edit… > “Add period with double-space”
2. Type any character (double space must be after a non-whitespace character)
3. tap "space" twice in quick succession.
4. See error

**Expectation**
`onValueChange` fired

**Environment**
- Slate Version: 0.117.0
- Operating System: macOS 14.5 (23F79)
- Browser: Chrome

issue_title: In Firefox ，When entering Japanese characters, the first letter will not be displayed after selection
issue_body:
**Description**

When inputting Japanese characters, the first letter does not display after selection. In Firefox, using the Japanese input method, inputting sinn and selecting up and down to 🆕 does not display the corresponding character on the page, and selecting other characters up and down also does not select
If you only see a drop-down option, you can press the left key and then the down key to display more options.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/78efe776-f088-4919-abf2-f0cb85c1979d

issue_title: [Android] `editor.insertText` inserts the text in the wrong place.
issue_body:
**Description**
After typing some text and then inserting content via `editor.insertText` the text is inserted in the wrong place.

**Recording**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/910a8a69-d266-40a3-aebb-958eebe4b2e8

**Sandbox**
https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/4hytdl

**Steps**
To reproduce the behavior:
1. Start typing in the Slate editor
2. Quite quickly do an action that calls the `editor.insertText` (in the sandbox the button in the bottom banner)
3. The `/` will be inserted before the last letter that you have typed

**Environment**
- Slate Version: 0.114.0
- Operating System: `Android 15; CPH2415 (OnePlus 10T) Build/AP3A.240617.008; 35; REL`
- Browser: `Chrome 136.0.7103.125` (up-to date at the time of writting)

Tested also on `Google Pixel 7a` and `Samsung Galaxy A54 5G` with the same browser version

issue_title: can't input Chinese characters IOS, but can input English characters.
issue_body:
**Description**
I used Safari on my iPhone to open the example(https://www.slatejs.org/examples/richtext) provided by Slate, and found that I could not input Chinese. The following is the error I got when I opened it with the iOS simulator. At the same time, I found that if I used English as the first word and Chinese as the subsequent words, it could be input normally.

**Recording**
When the editor value is empty, entering Chinese will result in an error and cannot be deleted.
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/61c6f15c-db26-46cd-a2b4-065771552009
Enter some English first, then enter Chinese, and you can enter and delete normally.
The following is the error information:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/882f3fd8-f7f1-497e-a6bb-2506713c690b)


**Expectation**
I hope that when the editor is empty, I can input Chinese normally and the console will not report an error.

**Environment**
- Slate Version: [0.103.0]
- Slate-react Version: [0.108.0]
- Operating System: [iOS 15.0]
- Browser: [Safari 17.2]

issue_title: redo and undo are wrong for insert operation records
issue_body:
**Description**
redo and undo are wrong for insert operation records. When Replace is selected, undo will also record the last insertion operation before insert, causing the last operation to be lost during undo.

**Recording**

In this case, I think "insert_text" 5, should not appear in the undo stack

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fd553457-72cf-430a-b1c8-c8bbdb4eacdb


In this case, I think the input box should not be cleared

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/781b97d8-b8e7-4969-8543-8db787657ac7

**Expectation**
I hope that when I perform the insert operation, what undo records is the data when I selected the operation in the last step.

issue_title: Update to immer@10+ breaks react native + hermes builds
issue_body:
**Description**
The update to `"immer": "^10.0.3"` in https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/pull/5528 prevents Slate from building in react native projects that utilize the hermes engine. The newer version of `immer` utilizes some features which are not implemented in [hermes yet](https://github.com/facebook/hermes/issues/685#issuecomment-1784172071).

We utilize Slate in react native to build and normalize documents that match our web application even though we don't use it to control an actual input element.

**Steps**
To reproduce the behavior:
1. Add Slate as a dependency to a React Native project
2. Enable hermes engine
3. Attempt a release build of the application

**Expectation**
I'm unsure if Slate should take any action here. We are working around the issue by pinning `immer` to `9.0.21` and forcing slate to use that version. This currently works because slate hasn't updated any usages of `immer` so it is still compatible with `9.0.21`.

This is largely an FYI issue for others. Additionally, resisting any `immer` 10+ backwards incompatible code might want to be avoided to continue supporting react native and older browser usages until strictly necessary.

**Environment**
- Slate Version: 0.102
pr_title: Remove immer, making the core `slate` package dependency-free

issue_title: `onValueChange` not triggered when selecting text from right to left then paste
issue_body:
**Description**
For slate react, `onValueChange` is not triggered when 
- select all content from **right to left**, then do a ctrl+v/cmd+v paste

**Recording**

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bebfaedd-19ea-474b-8c1d-2113845eb84a)

**Sandbox**
https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/slate-reproductions-forked-jc6pq2?workspaceId=ws_Pbq92KWBCGm3cg9fGwjBFV

**Steps**
To reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/slate-reproductions-forked-jc6pq2?workspaceId=ws_Pbq92KWBCGm3cg9fGwjBFV
2. Copy some random text in your clipboard
3. Select all text in slate editor from **right to left**,
4. Do a cmd+v/ctrl+v paste
5. The onValueChange callback is not triggered!
 
**Expectation**
Since pasting value does change the value, onValueChange should be triggered.

As a matter of fact, 
- select text from left to right, then paste
- cmd+a select all, then paste
works fine, only select from right to left then paste does not work XD

**Environment**
- Slate Version: latest (0.112)
- Operating System: macOS
- Browser: Chrome

issue_title: Pressing undo after pressing enter between a marked span(like bold) and normal text removes the normal text completely.
issue_body:
**Description**
Pressing undo after pressing enter between a marked span(like bold) and normal text removes the normal text completely.

**Recording**

https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/slate/assets/42038491/d578416e-987a-4782-8f03-9be61bb09487

**Sandbox**
It can be reproduced on the official site [https://www.slatejs.org/](https://www.slatejs.org/) 

**Steps**
To reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to [slate editor](https://www.slatejs.org/)
2. Enable bold and start entering some text.
3. Disable bold and enter space and then enter some text.
4. Bring the cursor back to the end of previous bold text and press **Enter**.
5. Press undo to see the second text getting removed completely instead of going back to same paragraph as before.

**Expectation**
Pressing undo should make the second text go back to same paragraph.

**Environment**
- Slate Version: latest
- Operating System: macOs Sonoma 14.1.1
- Browser: Chrome

issue_title: Linux update bugs
issue_body:
## tl;dr

The heavy testing I did related to the merge of #3077 led to the discovery of some Zui update issues on Linux. I've concluded that moving to the latest [electron-updater](https://yarnpkg.com/package?name=electron-updater) version along with a modest set of Zui code changes will address these issues and also put us in position to hopefully start doing auto-updates on Linux soon (#871).

## Background

On macOS and Windows our [mac-win-updater.ts](https://github.com/brimdata/zui/blob/main/apps/zui/src/domain/updates/mac-win-updater.ts) uses [electron-update's autoUpdater](https://www.electron.build/auto-update.html). Because that autoUpdater traditionally did not support Linux, we rolled our own [linux-updater.ts](https://github.com/brimdata/zui/blob/main/apps/zui/src/domain/updates/linux-updater.ts) to act as an "update helper" of sorts. It asks about the latest available release on GitHub by polling an endpoint of the [update.electronjs.org](https://github.com/electron/update.electronjs.org) service and, if the service responds indicating a newer release is available, the Zui user is shown a pop-up notification. If the user then clicks the **Install** button in the pop-up, the [brimdata.io Download page](https://www.brimdata.io/download/) page is opened in their browser (or the [Zui Insiders "latest release" page on GitHub](https://github.com/brimdata/zui-insiders/releases/latest) if they're running Insiders).

This has always been a little hacky because our code actually checks for a later _non-Linux_ release (specifically, macOS) on the assumption that a newer Linux artifact of that same release would also be present. This isn't 100% bulletproof, since it's possible for an Actions build job to fail part way through such that it produces usable artifacts for some platforms but not others. But it's generally been seen as better than just requiring Linux users to always manually check for newer releases.

The two problems with this approach described below are both related to this polling of the Electron update server:

https://github.com/brimdata/zui/blob/619f5d890edd8dbe8cf1e2c90ae3ebdcbd9b2b9d/apps/zui/src/domain/updates/linux-updater.ts#L35

## Problem 1 - The new artifact naming convention makes releases look ineligible as update targets

This was a detail of which I was previously unaware, but the way our Linux update helper polls the Electron update server endpoint relies on the existence of an artifact of the form `*(mac|darwin|osx).*(-arm).*.zip` to consider a release as an eligible update target. This can be seen if we manually construct a URL similar to the one our Linux update helper uses, but point at a repo that has no artifacts at all.

```
$ curl -w "%{http_code}" https://update.electronjs.org/brimdata/zui-docs-site/darwin-x64/1.2.3
No updates found (needs asset matching *(mac|darwin|osx).*(-arm).*.zip in public repository)404
```

Before the merge of #3077 (so, Zui Insiders release [v1.7.1-19](http

issue_title: "TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'isUnset')" when opening Detail view
issue_body:
Repro is with GA Zui tagged `v1.8.0` and at commit 52b4b58.

A user first reported the issue in a [community Slack thread](https://brimdata.slack.com/archives/C010DR0HHMF/p1719413291888149). In their own words:

> Can't open any packet after the recent update, I keep getting this error!
> Same issue on both Windows and Linux deb
>
>  ![image](https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/dd664ada-7f4d-45e3-98f1-1d1f5983ae4a)
> 
> Will try that but everything works fine except when I double click a packet. I created a new pool then loaded the pcap file then query after that when I double click on a packet, I get that error

I seem to have reproduced the issue on macOS as well, though I'd word the repro a little differently than the user. The problem doesn't seem to be "packet" specific, but rather, I was able to trigger it by applying `fuse` and then opening a record in the **Detail** view. Here's a repro in GA Zui tagged `v1.8.0` using the attached [ifconfig.pcapng.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15993903/ifconfig.pcapng.gz) test data (after uncompressing).

https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/332ca0e5-2de7-4e31-a30d-ba2a9c0ce8c6

I then did a binary search and confirmed the problem started happening at commit 52b4b58, which is associated with the changes in #3103. This next video is a repro at commit 52b4b58 using the attached [results.zng.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15993929/results.zng.gz) test data, which is just the records generated by loading that `ifconfig.pcapng`.

https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/0e92053f-e07f-468b-8237-42b14be0fd8c

Since this shows a more detailed error message I can start digging in and figure out what needs to be fixed. However, a secondary question is why the stack dump shown to the user in the GA release is so opaque. In this case I was lucky to have been able to make a lucky guess as to how to trigger the problem (the many `null` values in the background of the user's screenshot made me suspicious that `fuse` was involved) but it would have been much more helpful if the stack trace could have referenced the original lines of code.

issue_title: SIGSEGV crash when maximizing window on Linux
issue_body:
Repro is with Zui commit ca047c0. I bumped into this issue while going through the test permutations on all platforms before merging #3084.

The first repro video is taken with Zui Insiders release [1.7.1-12](https://github.com/brimdata/zui-insiders/releases/tag/v1.7.1-12) which is based on Zui commit ca047c0. As we can see, the app initially launches ok, but then when I double-click the title bar to maximize the window, the app window disappears due to a crash.

https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/2eb61814-4278-4bc8-9327-585f0d84725c

The second repro video is taken by starting Zui in Dev Mode at commit ca047c0. This more clearly shows the root cause of the crash being an Electron SIGSEGV.

https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/dd8806c2-33ca-4f80-a742-7b5fa8e3e6d7

That Zui commit ca047c0 happens to be related to #3061 where Zui's Electron dependency was advanced from `28.0.0` to `30.0.1`. In follow-on Dev Mode tests I advanced the Electron dependency one patch release at a time and found the problem went away at Electron `30.0.5`. Sure enough, there's an Electron issue https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/41839 that describes this exact symptom as having been fixed in [Electron release v30.0.5](https://github.com/electron/electron/releases/tag/v30.0.5), so we'll want to advance Zui's Electron dependency to a release like this one that includes the fix.

(Side note: I initially found this issue on Rocky Linux (RPM installer) after not having seen it on Ubuntu Linux (DEB installer) and therefore thought incorrectly at first that it was installer-related. Of course, in hindsight I can see the problem has to do with saved app state, since coincidentally on my Ubuntu VM I happened to have previously had Zui launched in a non-maximized state and didn't happen to have a reason to maximize it, so I didn't happen to bump into this bug in my testing there. By comparison, on my Rocky Linux VM I don't think I'd previously done any testing with Zui Insiders, so upon first launch the app window happened to be maximized, triggering the bug.)
