issue_title: "TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'getBoundingClientRect')" when resizing histogram after toggle
issue_body:
Repro is with Zui commit 361906b.

Repro steps as shown in the attached video:

1. Drag test data `sample.zng` from this repo into Zui
2. Click the toggle button to hide the histogram, then click it again to restore it
3. Click and drag the base of the histogram to start resizing it
4. Error `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'getBoundingClientRect')` appears

https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/4720778c-6d7c-4feb-a8ba-e725f0e02bb0

The error message that appears:

![image](https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/9c828971-f8cf-41c8-80fa-038e05e61867)
pr_title: Pass the Ref to the Histogram Resizer

issue_title: Pulling scrollbar to bottom not triggering load of more data
issue_body:
Repro is with Zui commit 8444565.

I've noticed that often if I grab the vertical scrollbar control and pull it all the way to the bottom, it doesn't automatically trigger the loading of more data. In the attached video I got more data on the first "pull", but not on the subsequent ones. Counterintuitively, once in that state, if I scroll _downward_ with my mouse roller (can't easily be shown in the video, but I'm doing it) it doesn't trigger the loading of more data, which gives me the impression there's no more available, but then if I scroll _upward_ with the roller it loads more.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5934157/228941352-3cf81388-7b4b-4c3f-8c86-1d4dc48b2416.mp4

See also the issue #2636. It seems we have a few things we could improve in terms of visual cues about how much data there is to view. The "Results: First ..." in the bottom of the window is doing some of this for us, but I find it to be fairly subtle.
pr_title: Use scrolltop and height to determine if we should load more

issue_title: New query during load of stacked bar chart causes "AbortError: BodyStreamBuffer was aborted"
issue_body: Repro is with Zui commit 28f3f50. This issue is most easily triggered with a large data set, so I've loaded these 24 files of GitHub data into a pool called `github`. Then I click on **Query Pool** and while the stacked bar chart still shows "Loading..." I issue the following query against that pool: ``` repo.name=="duckdb/duckdb" type=="PullRequestEvent" | count() by login:=actor.login | sort -r count | head 5 ``` As shown in the attached video, once that query is issued an Unhandled Runtime Error `AbortError: BodyStreamBuffer was aborted` is surfaced. https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/0b7a0590-6f67-420e-846a-c0a16f288c09 Via binary search I found that this problem appeared at Zui commit 28f3f50 (associated with #2934). At commit 4399c1a that came right before that, there was still an Uncaught DOMException shown in DevTools, but it did not surface in the app as an error. ![image](https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/00dd1745-509c-4b21-b1f2-80977bed065f) But then at commit 28f3f50 it looks like: ![image](https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/3511c930-62d8-4a02-ab45-3f5397c0c1d5) As @jameskerr said upon reviewing this evidence: > ok, so they got more specific about the error for some reason and that trigger our NextJS dev server to pop something up.
pr_title: Fix Bugs Discovered by Steve

issue_title: Meta-query returns "SyntaxError: Unexpected end of JSON input"
issue_body:
Repro is with Zui commit 21abbf6.

Steps shown in the attached video:

1. Load some sample data into Zui
2. Click **+** and **New Query Session**
3. Run the [meta-query](https://zed.brimdata.io/docs/commands/zed#meta-queries) `from :branches`

At Zui commit 21abbf6 (associated with the changes in #3085) the app shows the error "SyntaxError: Unexpected end of JSON input". At prior commit, ad5657d the meta-query result was successfully shown.

https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/73257cd6-1ba9-4892-b6fa-3985f4a69db4

I poked through the changes in #3085 and I think I see what's going on, which is also visible in the attached packet captures 
[ad5657d.pcapng.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15794191/ad5657d.pcapng.gz) and [21abbf6.pcapng.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15794193/21abbf6.pcapng.gz) that I took when performing this operation at the respective commits. If we **Follow TCP Stream** in Wireshark on each, for the ad5657d case, we see that the API activity halts after the app gets a response to its query `from :branches\n | count()\n  | { i: count(), v: this}\n  | i > 0\n  | head 500\n  | yield v`.

![image](https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/cfdc1ad4-9329-49e5-9be9-767201b7ad72)

Whereas at commit 21abbf6 that same first query result comes back much the same, but then the app marches on and issues a query `from undefined | min(ts), max(ts)` which comes back with an `HTTP 400 Bad Request`.

![image](https://github.com/brimdata/zui/assets/5934157/070e2b45-5fbd-43b1-9b70-41ae79d94b79)

And based on my added console logging, it looks like this is getting handled as a `DefaultError` at this line:

https://github.com/brimdata/zui/blob/8cec81d3e64e73fdcf84273642eb31d348f5830e/apps/zui/src/views/results-pane/error.tsx#L11
pr_title: Only Render Histogram if you Can

issue_title: [Bug] Bold Formatting Not Reflected in Editor UI
issue_body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [x] Yes, I have searched the existing issues and none of them match my problem.

### Product Variant

Cloud (https://rxresu.me)

### Describe the bug you're experiencing

### Summary
When applying **bold formatting** to selected text in the Experience summary editor, the editor does not visually display the text as bold. However, after saving, the final rendered output correctly shows the text in bold.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Add an experience in the experience section.
2. Select a portion of text in the description.
3. Click the **Bold** option.
4. Observe that the selected text does not visually appear bold in the editor.
5. Save the content.
6. View the saved content.

### Expected Behavior
The selected text should immediately appear bold within the editor after applying the formatting.

### Actual Behavior
- The editor does not visually reflect bold formatting.
- After saving, the final displayed content correctly renders the text in bold.

### Impact
- Formatting cannot be verified in real time.
- Requires repeated cycles (bold → save → edit) to confirm formatting.
- Degrades editing experience and productivity.

### Reproducibility
Consistently reproducible.

### What template are you using?

Rhyhorn

### Anything else?

_No response_
pr_title: fix: bold formatting not visible in rich text editor
app_description: Reactive Resume is a free and open-source resume builder that simplifies the process of creating, updating, and sharing your resume.

issue_title: [Bug] Credentials(Username/Email + Password) sign-in returns 200 but no session cookie is set (stays on /auth/login)
issue_body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [x] Yes, I have searched the existing issues and none of them match my problem.

### Product Variant

Self-Hosted

### Describe the bug you're experiencing

### Summary
Self-hosted v5 (main): **signup works**, but **username+password** and **email+password** sign-in do not log in. UI briefly refreshes/re-renders and remains on `http://localhost:3000/auth/login` with **no error shown**.

### Expected behavior
Successful sign-in sets an authenticated session and redirects to the app.

### Actual behavior
- Click **Sign In** → page refresh/re-render → still on `/auth/login`
- No toast/error
- API calls return **200**
- Session remains unauthenticated

### Steps to reproduce
1. Deploy self-hosted v5 main.
2. Register a new account successfully.
3. Go to `/auth/login`.
4. Sign in with valid **username+password** (also repro with **email+password**).
5. Observe: no redirect; still logged out.

### What template are you using?

None

### Anything else?

### Network evidence
#### POST /api/auth/sign-in/username
- Status: **200**
- Response header contains only:
  - `Set-Cookie: better-auth.dont_remember=; Max-Age=0; Path=/; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax`
- **Missing** `Set-Cookie: better-auth.session_token=...`

#### GET /api/auth/get-session (immediately after)
- Status: **200**
- Same `dont_remember` cookie header; **no session cookie set**
- Session stays unauthenticated
pr_title: fix(auth): avoid dropping session_token Set-Cookie on sign-in

issue_body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [x] Yes, I have searched the existing issues and none of them match my problem.

### Product Variant

Cloud (https://rxresu.me)

### Describe the bug you're experiencing

When adding or editing work experience entries, the formatting in the Description field (bold text) is lost after saving. This makes it difficult to highlight key details such as project names, technologies, or responsibilities in a clear and professional way.

Steps to Reproduce:
Add or edit a work experience entry.
Apply formatting (e.g., bold text) in the Description field.
Save the entry.
Observe that the formatting is stripped, leaving only plain text.

In older versions of Reactive Resume, formatting was preserved correctly.
After returning to update my CV after a long time, I noticed that formatting is now lost.
This regression reduces the professional presentation of resumes and makes it harder to highlight important details.

Rhyhorn

issue_title: Images fail to load in articles using non-standard lazy-loading tags (e.g., WeChat data-src)
issue_body: ### Describe the Bug When capturing articles from certain platforms (specifically WeChat Official Accounts), images fail to display in the KaraKeep reader/preview view. Upon inspecting the DOM, I found that these images use non-standard attributes like data-src or data-srv for lazy loading, instead of the standard src attribute. KaraKeep's parser saves the original tag but doesn't seem to map these attributes to src, causing the browser to ignore the image source. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Capture a WeChat article (e.g., https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/kvhc3xjpBl8Uk4-Yv0-CAw). 2. Open the bookmark in KaraKeep. 3. Observe that images are broken or replaced by placeholders. 4. Inspect the image element (which can't be seen without inspecting) to see without a valid src attribute. ### Expected Behaviour The parser should identify common lazy-loading attributes (like data-src, data-srv, etc.) and rewrite them to the standard src attribute during the article cleaning process. or make it able to be seen in KaraKeep. ### Screenshots or Additional Context ### Device Details _No response_ ### Exact Karakeep Version v0.30.0 ### Environment Details _No response_ ### Debug Logs _No response_ ### Have you checked the troubleshooting guide? - [x] I have checked the troubleshooting guide and I haven't found a solution to my problem
pr_title: fix(crawler): normalize lazy-load img attributes to src before Readability

At first I thought the import was being aborted immediately because of the number of bookmarks and folders, but based on Karakeep’s error message and the subsequent debugging via Chrome DevTools, I found out it was because I had a bookmark folder with no name. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to http://localhost:3000/settings/import 2. Click on "Import" and select the bookmarks HTML file 3. See that the import gets immediately aborted and an error message is shown ### Expected Behaviour The bookmarks are imported normally after selecting the file. If the folder doesn’t have a name, it’s automatically replaced with “Unnamed.” ### Screenshots or Additional Context The error message: My debugging of the error: Here’s the corresponding snippet from the HTML file that causes the error. `

Bluetooth Fernbedienung

VOCOMO - Bluetooth HiFi-Verstärker & Freisprecheinrichtungen für BMW, VW, Mini, Ford, Opel nachrüsten - Bluetooth Fernbedienung (BLE Media Button)` ### Device Details Chrome 144.0.7559.31 beta (64-bit) on Windows 10 ### Exact Karakeep Version v0.29.3 ### Have you checked the troubleshooting guide? - [x] I have checked the troubleshooting guide and I haven't found a solution to my problem

issue_title: TWZ RSS feed fetch succeeds but imports 0 entries in v0.31.0
issue_body:
### Describe the Bug

  Karakeep fetches `https://www.twz.com/feed` successfully, but processes it as `0 entries`, so nothing imports.

  This is not a network failure:
  - the feed returns `200 OK`
  - KaraKeep marks the fetch as `success`
  - the worker log shows the feed was fetched successfully

  But after fetching, KaraKeep logs:
  - `Found 0 entries in feed "The War Zone"`
  - `No entries found.`

  As a result:
  - no new bookmarks are created
  - no new `rssFeedImports` rows are added
  - the feed appears healthy in the UI even though nothing is imported

### Steps to Reproduce

  1. Add the RSS feed `https://www.twz.com/feed`
  2. Open `Settings > Feeds`
  3. Trigger `Fetch now` for the feed
  4. Check the feed status and logs
  5. Observe that the fetch succeeds, but KaraKeep reports `0 entries` and imports nothing

### Expected Behaviour

  Karakeep should detect the RSS `<item>` entries from `https://www.twz.com/feed` and import new bookmarks normally.

### Screenshots or Additional Context

  Additional context:

  - Exact feed URL: `https://www.twz.com/feed`
  - The feed XML is valid and contains normal RSS `<item>` elements with fields like:
    - `title`
    - `link`
    - `guid`
    - `pubDate`
    - `category`

  I also tested:
  - disabling category/tag import for the feed
  - re-running `Fetch now`

  That did not resolve the issue. The worker still reported `Found 0 entries`.

  Possible parser clue:

  While debugging, I noticed that TWZ feed items include category data that may not match a simple `string[]` assumption. If KaraKeep is validating parsed items too strictly, that may explain why the feed is fetched
  successfully but all items are discarded and the worker reports `Found 0 entries`.

  I do not know if this is the exact root cause in upstream code, but it seems like the failure is happening after fetch and before import, during RSS item parsing/normalization.


### Device Details

Truenas 25.10

### Exact Karakeep Version

 v0.31.0

### Environment Details

  Docker Compose stack managed through Portainer on TrueNAS SCALE, behind reverse proxy/public hostname. Also tested directly via LAN URL.

### Debug Logs

  Relevant worker log output:

  ```text
  [feed][56372] Starting fetching feed "The War Zone" (y1cwipc28507bzkvn6myrlmk) ...
  [feed][56372] Successfully fetched feed "The War Zone" (y1cwipc28507bzkvn6myrlmk) ...
  [feed][56372] Found 0 entries in feed "The War Zone" (y1cwipc28507bzkvn6myrlmk) ...
  [feed][56372] No entries found.
  [feed][56372] Completed successfully
```


  Database state after rerun:

  Feed: The War Zone
  Enabled: true
  Import tags: false
  Last fetched status: success
  Last fetched at: 2026-03-06 18:01:44 local

  rssFeedImports count for active TWZ feed: 361
  Last import time: 2026-01-14 19:00:05 local

  This indicates the feed fetch is succeeding, but KaraKeep is not recognizing any entries as importable.

### Have you checked the troubleshooting guide?

- [x] I have checked
pr_title: fix(workers): handle TWZ RSS category objects

issue_title: Tag variable mentioned in the docs "$tags" does not work. "$Tags" does work (maybe)
issue_body:
### Describe the Bug

The [documentation](https://docs.karakeep.app/configuration/environment-variables/#inference-configs-for-automatic-tagging) says:
> You can use the placeholders $tags, $aiTags, $userTags in the prompt.

When I use "$tags" in a custom tagging rule, AI tagging does not work. I tried "$Tags" and tagging does work. This behavior changed in some way in 0.31. 

While troubleshooting this issue I noticed that in the Prompt Preview section of AI settings it says:
> Preview the actual prompts sent to AI based on your settings

Is what is shown in the prompt preview _actually_ what is sent at the prompt? The tag variables are not expanded in the preview, so either the tag variables aren't really working, or what is shown in the preview isn't what is actually sent. 

Are the API requests logged anywhere for debugging? I was wondering if the "$tags" variable possibly wasn't being expanded properly in the API request, but I don't see any way to view the actual prompt being sent.  

### Steps to Reproduce

Create custom tagging rules using "$tags". Example:

1. Check if any of the previously used tags are relevant for this content. Reuse them when appropriate to maintain consistency.
2. Here are the previously used tags that you should prioritize when appropriate: $tags

### Expected Behaviour

Tags should be created

_No response_

issue_title: Code with // comments pasted inside code blocks is incorrectly recognized as links
issue_body:
### Description

When pasting code into a code block in plate.js, any code snippet that contains `//` comments is incorrectly recognized and rendered as links.

Expected behavior: content inside a code block should only be highlighted according to syntax highlighting rules, without applying any Markdown formatting such as links.

This bug makes code snippets unreadable and breaks the expected behavior of code blocks.

### Reproduction URL

_No response_

### Reproduction steps

```bash
1. Insert a code block.
2. Paste the following snippet (or any other code):

// this is a comment
console.log("hello world");

3. Actual result: the entire pasted code (starting from //) is rendered as links.
4. Expected result: the code should remain plain code with syntax highlighting, without being transformed into links.
```

### Plate version

49.0.0

### Slate React version

0.117.0

### Screenshots

```bash

```

### Logs

```bash

```

### Browsers

_No response_
pr_title: Fix // paste being autolinked in code blocks
diff_preview:
diff --git a/.changeset/link-double-slash-patch.md b/.changeset/link-double-slash-patch.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..948afab725
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.changeset/link-double-slash-patch.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+---
+'@platejs/link': patch
+---
+
+- Fixed link validation so text starting with `//` is no longer treated as an internal path. This stops comment-style paste content from being autolinked by mistake, including inside code blocks.
diff --git a/.claude/docs/plans/2026-03-28-issue-4613-code-block-paste-must-not-autolink.md b/.claude/docs/plans/2026-03-28-issue-4613-code-block-paste-must-not-autolink.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..ff7787e4dc
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.claude/docs/plans/2026-03-28-issue-4613-code-block-paste-must-not-autolink.md
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+# Issue 4613: code block paste must not autolink
+
+## Tracker
+
+- Source type: GitHub issue
+- Source id: `#4613`
+- Title: `Code with // comments pasted inside code blocks is incorrectly recognized as links`
+- URL: `https://github.com/udecode/plate/issues/4613`
+- Task type: bug
+- Expected outcome: pasting plain code into an existing code block keeps the pasted text as plain code lines, without link wrapping or markdown-style link transforms
+- Browser surface: likely no; the stable seam should be editor insert handlers and package tests
+
+## Relevant area
+
+- `packages/code-block/src/lib/withInsertDataCodeBlock.ts`
+- `packages/link/src/lib/withLink.ts`
+- `packages/core/src/lib/plugins/ParserPlugin.ts`
+- existing code-block and link paste specs
+
+## Learnings
+
+- Recent code-block regressions in this repo were caused by structural assumptions around `code_line` nodes and stale decorate lifecycles
+- This bug smells like the paste pipeline is still allowing link auto-insert logic to run while the selection is inside a code block
+
+## Plan
+
+1. Trace the paste path for plain-text clipboard data inside code blocks.
+2. Add the smallest regression test at the real seam.
+3. Implement the minimal guard or transform to keep code-block paste as plain text.
+4. Run targeted tests, then package build, typecheck, and `lint:fix`.
+
+## Progress
+
+- Fetched the issue and comments.
+- Read repo instructions, task skill, planning-with-files, learnings-researcher, and tdd skill docs.
+- Read recent local learnings for code-block redecorate and structural line regressions.

issue_title: AIMenu: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0') during streaming
issue_body:
### Description

I'm generating long form (more than 3000 word) content using openai api with streaming enabled. 
Streaming is getting started, but in between while the client waits for the messages from Openai, it breaks the flow with the below exception. 

Open ai model - o4-mini
maxTokens - 100000

```
React.useEffect(() => {
    if (streaming) {
      const anchor = api.aiChat.node({ anchor: true }); 
      setTimeout(() => {
        const anchorDom = editor.api.toDOMNode(anchor![0])!;   // ---> failing here
        setAnchorElement(anchorDom);
      }, 0);
    }
    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
  }, [streaming]);
```



### Reproduction URL

_No response_

### Reproduction steps

```bash
1. Create a Plate editor with Markdown & AI plugin
2. In the api/ai/command, configure streaming with below model configuration
Model - o4-mini
maxToken - 100000
3. Set the prompt to write a blog post with minimum 3000 words.
```

### Plate version

49.0.2

### Slate React version

latest

### Screenshots

```bash

```

### Logs

```bash

```

### Browsers

_No response_

issue_title: resetNode does not respect idKey option
issue_body:
### Description

Hi,

When the nodeId plugin is configured with a specific `idKey` other than `"id"` the `resetBlock` function does not respect that and thus remove the `idKey` prop.

https://github.com/udecode/plate/blob/f1e9d1390456f415f59071caf21c782c9212b7ec/packages/core/src/lib/plugins/slate-extension/transforms/resetBlock.ts#L19


### Reproduction URL

_No response_

### Reproduction steps

```bash
Create a basic Plate setup and configure `idKey` to `"key"` for example.
Then try to reset and node
-> The `key` prop get removed
```

### Plate version

49

### Slate React version

0.118.1

### Screenshots

```bash

```

### Logs

```bash

```

### Browsers

Chrome

issue_title: [Bug]: Chips spacing weird in some cases
issue_body:
### Requirements

- [X] I checked the troubleshooting section in the README to verify that I have the latest Mushroom version.
- [X] I did a search to see if there is a similar issue or if a pull request is open.
- [X] I have read the state of card mod support (in pin issues) and understand card mod support is not provided in this repository.

### Current Behavior

In some situations, the chips in a chips card are weirdly aligned. This stems from the chips being separated with margins instead of the `gap` property. See the screenshot below:

![image](https://github.com/piitaya/lovelace-mushroom/assets/6919894/3bd07d08-a839-4cdd-92c3-99d3fe79c8c5)


### Expected Behavior

I would've expected no margin on the right side of the first chip.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Use the new grid dashboard layout
2. Play around with different chips alignments, sizes and counts
3. Observe margin issues.

### Context

YAML state
```yaml

```


### Environment

```markdown
- Browser: All evergreens (Edge, Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
- HA Version: 2024.7
- Mushroom: 3.6.2
```


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: [Bug]: Visual editor not working for service calls with target floor_id
issue_body:
### Requirements

- [X] I checked the troubleshooting section in the README to verify that I have the latest Mushroom version.
- [X] I did a search to see if there is a similar issue or if a pull request is open.
- [X] I have read the state of card mod support (in pin issues) and understand card mod support is not provided in this repository.

### Current Behavior

When creating a call-service action like light.turn_off I can pick areas but when picking a floor it will show the error:
![image](https://github.com/piitaya/lovelace-mushroom/assets/96917/b77650ca-78ee-45b8-a8d0-6de41ddbe7d9)


### Expected Behavior

The editor should allow the floor_id. From my research so far it looks like this object is loaded from the `home-assistant-js-websocket` npm package but it was added in a version newer to the one included in mushroom. So updating that dependency would fix the problem.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Create a floor in home assistant with a room
2. Create a new mushroom card (e.g. entity card)
3. Change tap action to service call and pick the `light.turn_off` service
4. Select " + Choose area" and in the dropdown pick the floor you created
5. Visual editor will jump to yaml mode and show warning:
![image](https://github.com/piitaya/lovelace-mushroom/assets/96917/c677d235-2d59-40ac-a01e-58895fd2469b)


### Context

YAML state
```yaml

```


### Environment

```markdown
- Browser: Edge
- HA Version: 2024.5.3
- Mushroom: 3.5.4
```


### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Bug: Timezone handling for event invites is incorrect
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
When you receive an event invite, the time displayed is in the _senders_ local time, but the timezone displayed is the _received_ timezone.

For example, if I, as the sender am in Bulgaria (EEST (UTC+3)), and I send an invite for an event at 4pm EEST to a recipient in the UK (BST (UTC+1)), the recipient should see the invite time as 2pm BST. However instead it displays 4pm BST (the senders time, but with BST as the timezone).

Have logged into GMail and confirmed that, in GMail, the correct time is displayed (2pm BST):

![Incorrect time shown in Mailspring](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10179637/83633001-1ee29800-a598-11ea-84c4-0d71dc7fce13.png)

![Correct time shown on GMail](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10179637/83633043-36218580-a598-11ea-97c8-b3cfdf54eb61.png)


**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Send an invite (likely _not_ by using Mailspring) from one timezone (e.g. EST)
2. Receive the invite via Mailspring with your setup to use a different timezone from the original invite (e.g. PST)
3. See the time as being the invite time from the sender, but suffixed with your local timezone.

**Expected behavior**
Either a) The time should be shown using the senders time _and_ timezone (not ideal), or the time should be showng and converted to your timezone (preferable).

**Screenshots**
See above

**Setup: (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora
 - OS Fedora release 28 (Twenty Eight)
 - Mailspring Version: 1.7.6

**Additional context** 
Have only tested _receiving_ invites into a GSuite/GMail account -  haven't tested sending them, or seeing if the same issue exists when receiving invites through other mail providers, although I cannot see why it wouldn't.

issue_title: Bug: Incorrect time when receiving Zoom meetings
issue_body:
It appears that when I receive a Zoom meeting request from someone in a different timezone, the time for that meeting is incorrect when I read the invite in Mailspring, but when I review my calendar (Google), the meeting is scheduled correctly.  To be honest, I've not noticed this happen with other meeting providers, but I will keep an eye out for them.  

In the attached image, the scheduled time for the meeting is 9am PDT but as you can see, it appears as 11am PDT in Mailspring. When I review my Google calendar, it appears as 9am PDT. The email originated from someone located in the Central time zone, two hours ahead of my (Pacific) time zone.

![Screenshot from 2020-05-26 11-47-57](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7572742/82940057-20cdab00-9f49-11ea-905e-a5a3a1c0306c.png)


**Setup: (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 18 Desktop
 - Mailspring Version: ex: 1.7.8 (13635bcf)

issue_title: Fastmail: Clarify that password should be IMAP
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**

Fastmail supports both IMAP and JMAP.

Mail spring does not specify which protocol it uses, so when creating an app password one has to guess. Being that JMAP is led by fastmail, and the recommended  approach, one would assume that's what mailspring supports.

After generating and providing the password to mailspring, it throws an error message indicating "Check your username and password (IMAP).

It would be best to clarify what protocol mailspring will use **before** a users provides a password, since one has to chose the protocol before generating the password.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open mailspring
2. Log into mailspring
3. Select fastmail -- you're prompted for credentials
4. Head over to fastmail to generate an app password.
5. A protocol must be selected at this point. Mailspring does not indicate any, so let's assume JMAP.
6. Finish generating the app password.
7. Put the password in mailspring.
8. At this point, mailspring show an error and clarifies that I should have selected IMAP in step 5.

**Expected behavior**
Supported protocol should be shown _before_ a password is provided, since it's required to generate a password.

**Actual behaviour**
Supported protocol should be shown _after_ a password is provided, so the initial password must be deleted and another one must be generated.

**Setup: (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: ArchLinux
 - Mailspring Version: 1.7.8-13635bcf

**Additional context** 

This is more of a UX issue than anything else -- it's not a blocker, but just annoying.

Follow %@ to create one and then paste it below.', + 'FastMail requires that you create a unique app password of type IMAP for email apps like Mailspring.   -- Please use https://community.getmailspring.com/ for all bug reports

## Objective
When opening an old e-mail whose mail body is not loaded (not in the local cache?), the unsubcsribe link that is added to the e-mail view crashes with the following error.

issue_title: Bug: Can launch minimized in Notification Area
issue_body:
I don't know if this is a bug or maybe a request for a new feature. 

I want Mailspring to launch at startup and minimized (in the background) to the notification area in Windows 10. At the moment it always starts open in my screen. I then have to click 'x' to send it to the notification area.

I've tried 'run minimized' in the properties of the shortcut but that doesn't work. I've also created a task in Task Scheduler to '\background' but still it doesn't stop it launching.

I may be missing something but it would be nice for it to go straight to the notification area at startup. 

Thanks for a great email client!

Running: Mailspring 1.7.6 (latest version)
Windows 10 Home
pr_title: Ensure background start works in maximized mode

issue_title: Bug: Can't install on Ubuntu 20.10
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
The deb package depends on `gvfs-bin`, this package has long been deprecated and is no longer available in Ubuntu 20.10.
I can not see that any `gvfs-*` command line tools is used by mailspring so it might be as easy as just dropping `gvfs-bin` from https://github.com/Foundry376/Mailspring/blob/master/app/build/resources/linux/debian/control.in

Electron itself used `gvfs-bin` in version 2 which is why it might have been included in the first place, but since version 3 it has migrated to the newer and supported gio tools https://github.com/atom/atom/issues/17452#issuecomment-490037674

issue_title: File field upload widget javascript onclick handler is broken in 1.5.0
issue_body:
The file upload field widget seems to be broken in 1.5.0. The error is in the onclick javascript handler for the upload button not being able to find the hidden `<input type=file ...>` field in the DOM hierarchy next to the button using the DOM traversal combinator provided in the `onclick` attribute of the button.

This is a client-side DOM traversal error.

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Upgrade to 1.5.0
2. Try to use any edit view with file upload field to upload a file
3. Look for error messages in the browser console, specifically ones like `"null is not a valid element..."`
4. Downgrade to 1.4.2

I have also tried removing the field from the edit view and adding it anew but it didn't change the wrong behaviour.
pr_title: fix upload custom button in popup. Fixes #3859

issue_title: Users table Read/Write roles default to admin on Backup Restore
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
When restoring a Backup to a fresh installation, the Minimum role to read/write for the Users table defaults to "admin", even if the Backup has the values set to something else

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create an instance and set the Read/Write permissions for the Users table to something other than admin
2. Perform a Backup
3. Create another instance and restore the Backup to it
4. Observe that the Read/Write permissions are set to admin, regardless of what was set before the Backup

**Expected behavior**
Read/Write permissions should be set according to the backed up values
pr_title: Restore user table properties. Drop table constraints on clear-all

issue_title: Dynamic button based survey triggers are not working
issue_body: ### Issue Summary The engineering team at workera has investigated an issue related to actions not triggering a Formbricks survey. After enabling the debug query param we see that the event listeners are attached at the beginning of a page load mean means that any dynamic button that get's added at a later stage wont be setup with a click listener and trigger a survey to show. This is most likely due to the listeners being setup using document.querySelectorAll().forEach(el => el.addEventListener("click", fn) rather than listening for all click events and filtering our event targets which match the CSS selector. According to your documentation (https://formbricks.com/docs/xm-and-surveys/surveys/website-app-surveys/actions#1-click-action) "A Click Action is triggered when a user clicks on a specific element within your application. You can define the element’s inner text, CSS selector or both to trigger the survey." It does not say that dynamic content is not supported, or that it needs to exist at page load. With this we are considering this as a bug. I know we can work around this by implementing in code a click listener and fire a custom action event, but this is more of a short-term ad-hock solution, not a fix to the root problem. ### Expected Behavior Listening for all click events and filtering our event targets which match the CSS selector. ### Other information (incl. screenshots, Formbricks version, steps to reproduce,...) ### Your Environment Self-hosted Formbricks
pr_title: fix(js-core): use closest() fallback for nested click target matching

issue_title: Multi-language survey buttons fallback to English after answering a question
issue_body: ### Issue Summary As an admin I create a multi-language survey which supports Hungarian and English languages. I add a welcome card and two or more blocks of questions. I set Hungarian as default language and I activate the language selector. As a responder I start answering the survey. The welcome card is displayed in Hungarian including my text and button labels, Formbricks brand. I click on the "Következő" ("Next" in English) button. The first block is displayed in Hungarian including my text and the button labels, Formbricks brand. I select an answer and click on the "Következő" ("Next in English") button. The next block is displayed in Hungarian but with English button labels and "Powered by Formbricks" text. I have to use the language selector and select Hungarian again to change the button labels and Formbricks brand back to Hungarian. ### Expected Behavior The survey should be displayed in Hungarian from the start to the end. ### Other information (incl. screenshots, Formbricks version, steps to reproduce,...) Formbricks version 4.8.0 ### Your Environment Self-hosted Formbricks
pr_title: fix:  (Duplicate) prevent multi-language survey buttons from falling back to English

issue_title: Multi-language surveys broken in v4.8.0 (AGPL)
issue_body:
### Issue Summary

https://github.com/formbricks/formbricks/pull/7426 released with v4.8.0 moved the multi-language survey support from enterprise to the open-source (AGPL) version of Formbricks (Thank you!).

After updating to v4.8.0, I've followed the [docs](https://formbricks.com/docs/xm-and-surveys/surveys/general-features/multi-language-surveys#creating-a-multi-language-survey) to enable multi-language support in a survey, but got stuck at step 3 ("Enable Multi-language Support"): When enabling the <kbd>Multiple Languages</kbd> toggle in the survey editor, nothing happens (card doesn't expand).

Maybe because some required unsafe JS is blocked by the CSP? In the browser console I see the following error referencing `util.js:157:13` (`turbopack:///[project]/node_modules/zod/v4/core/util.js`):

```
Content-Security-Policy: The page’s settings blocked a JavaScript eval (script-src) from being executed because it violates the following directive: “script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https:” (Missing 'unsafe-eval')
```

### Expected Behavior

Enabling the <kbd>Multiple Languages</kbd> toggle in the survey editor works.

### Other information (incl. screenshots, Formbricks version, steps to reproduce,...)

Formbricks v4.8.0

### Your Environment

Self-hosted Formbricks

issue_title: POST /api/v2/organizations/{organizationId}/users returns 500 when recreating a previously deleted user
issue_body:
### Issue Summary

Summary
-------
On a self-hosted Formbricks instance, creating a user through the Management API returns `500 Internal Server Error` for an email address that previously existed and was deleted manually through the UI.

The issue was reproduced both:
- from a synchronization script
- and manually with a direct API call


Environment
-----------
- Formbricks version: `4.8.1`
- Deployment mode: self-hosted with Docker
- Authentication: OIDC / SSO managed externally
- API used: `POST /api/v2/organizations/{organizationId}/users`


Problem Description
-------------------
If a user existed in Formbricks, was later deleted manually through the Formbricks UI, and is then recreated through the API with the same email address, the API returns `500 Internal Server Error`.

At the same time:
- `GET /api/v2/organizations/{organizationId}/users` does not return that user anymore
- but the backend logs show a unique constraint failure on the `email` field

So from the API client point of view, the user looks deleted and absent, but the backend still considers the email as already existing.


### Expected Behavior

Expected Behavior
-----------------
Ideally, one of these behaviors would be preferable:
- allow recreating a previously deleted user with the same email, or
- expose deleted/inactive users consistently in the API so the client can reactivate or reuse them, or
- return a clear conflict response explaining that the email still exists internally
It should not return a generic `500 Internal Server Error`.


### Other information (incl. screenshots, Formbricks version, steps to reproduce,...)

Steps to Reproduce
------------------
1. Create a user in Formbricks.
2. Delete that user manually through the Formbricks UI.
3. Call:
   `POST /api/v2/organizations/{organizationId}/users`
4. Use the same email address as the deleted user.


Example Request
---------------
Note: For privacy reasons, hostnames, organization ids, names, and email addresses have been replaced with `redacted`.

```bash
curl -X POST 'https://redacted/api/v2/organizations/redacted/users' \
  -H 'x-api-key: redacted' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "email": "redacted",
    "name": "redacted",
    "role": "member"
  }'
```

Backend Logs
------------
Relevant backend logs from the Formbricks Docker container:

```text
POST /api/v2/organizations/redacted/users

error:
Invalid `prisma.user.create()` invocation:

Unique constraint failed on the fields: (`email`)
```

In our case, the request is not trying to create a brand new email address from scratch. It is trying to recreate an email address that belonged to a user previously deleted through the UI.


Additional Observations
-----------------------
- `GET /api/v2/organizations/{organizationId}/users` does not return that user anymore
- `POST /api/v2/organizations/{organizationId}/users` still fails on unique email constraint
- this suggests an inconsistency between the user listing behavior and the
pr_title: fix(api): return 409 Conflict instead of 500 when creating user with duplicate email

issue_title: [MdTable] md-sort not applied on initial render
issue_body:
Given the following example: https://vuematerial.io/components/table/

Scroll down to the table sample for Sort, and the md-sort property is set to Name, as indicated by the arrow adjacent to the Name column header.

The data however is unsorted (the sequence being IDs 1,2,3,4,5 as in the underlying dataset). Click the column header to actually apply a sort by Name.

Thus the md-sort property is only enabling the sort facility, but on first load, whilst the directional arrow is drawn to indicate that the data is being sorted, the data is not actually sorted until the column header is clicked, rather it remains unsorted and is displayed in the same sequence as it exists in the underlying array.

Screenshot for clarity.

![screen_shot_2018-05-23_at_13_46_27](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2006224/40425398-26e22332-5e90-11e8-8e73-de4b9fea3f04.jpg)

This is using 1.0.0-beta-10.2.

This is my first time raising an issue, apologies if I have got anything wrong and my huge thanks to all involved for their great work.
pr_title: fix(MdTable): sort on created
app_description: Material Design for Vue.js

issue_title: [MdDatepicker] Positioning error when placeing the datepicker into an dynamic layout item
issue_body:
### Steps to reproduce

I've placed the datepicker into a responsive formular. The dom element that contains the datepicker has an dynamic percentage width value (like md-large-size-15 or style="width:15%;": The percentage value triggers this issue). When the datepicker dialog will be rendered, the top position will be set on "top: -149px;" and outside the viewport. This issue only exists in version 1.0.0-beta-10.x. In version  1.0.0-beta-8 the datepicker is placed correctly.

You can see the behavior in my CodeSandbox link below.

### Which browser?

This can be reproduced with latest version of Vue (2.5.16), Vue-Material 1.0.0-beta-10.2. 
Tested in the following Broswers: Google Chrome (Version 66.0.3359.117), latest Safari

### What is expected?

The initial calculated height value for the MdDatepickerDialog is wrong. The "setContentStyles" uses the element.offsetHeight value. This value changes after the transition is completed. If the transition hook is changed from @enter to @after-enter the issue is solved. But than the height of the displayed dialog ist to small.

### What is actually happening?

The popper.js calculates a wrong top position. The element is placed outside the viewport and expands to the bottom of the viewport: See the CodeSandbox link below.

### Reproduction Link

https://codesandbox.io/s/2wro15pjmy
pr_title: fix(MdDatepicker): positioning when used inside flex container
app_description: Material Design for Vue.js

### Steps to reproduce

Create table with elements that can be sorted by id and other types, and sort them in a way that breaks the linearity of the id numbers. Once the select all checkbox is clicked, only some are selected.
Edit - There seems to be a litany of issues with select all, the same issue also appears and is reproducable in the codepen below with many other cases,
- By searching for specific elements and selecting all, not all are selected (e.g. search for 't' and try to select all elements https://i.imgur.com/YHyIUlS.png
- Exit out of that search and again try to select all on all elements, all of a sudden a few elements are not selected when clicking the select all. https://i.imgur.com/3ELZgb2.png

### Which browser?

Vue-Material@^1.0.0-beta-7
Vue@^2.5.0
Electron@1.7.10

### What is expected?

It should select all elements regardless of id order

### What is actually happening?

I believe it is using the id's to decide which elements to select, and when it encounters out of order id's it goes off.

### Reproduction Link

https://codepen.io/anon/pen/RxKMxW
Selecting all when sorted by id works fine, but when sorting by name or anything else that breaks the order of the ID column, not all elements are selected, only around half. https://i.imgur.com/pSTfj6N.png
pr_title: fix(MdTable): reactive selection
app_description: Material Design for Vue.js

issue_title: [MdListItem] Clickable MdListItem hides its action button's click
issue_body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a list containing a `<md-list-item/>`.
2. Add a click handler to that list item: `<md-list-item @click="console.log('clicked')"/>`.
3. Add a button as an action to that item:
   ```js
   <md-list-item @click="console.log('clicked')">
     <md-button class="md-list-action" @click="console.log('action')"/>
   </md-list-item>
   ```
4. Try to click the list item's action button.

### Which browser?

Firefox 59.0.2
(it *works* in Chrome 65.0.3325.181)

### What is expected?

The button reacts to hover and triggers its click action.

### What is actually happening?

Only the list item's "selection" is triggered. The button displays neither a hover effect, nor is its click action called.
<!-- Is there anything else we should know? -->

### Reproduction Link

https://codesandbox.io/s/p4y4ykxo7

issue_title: [MdField] don't function correctly in IE11
issue_body:
Input fields in IE11 don't seem to get styled properly.  If you click away from the field (blur) after typing a few characters,  The label enlarges back again and the field value is hidden. 

The cause seems to be that, unlike other browsers,  the field doesn't get the `md-has-value` class when the field has a value.  It is:

```
<div class="md-field md-theme-default md-focused">
  <label for="card-number">16 digit card number</label>
  <input class="md-input" id="card-number" type="text">
/div>
```

instead of 

```
<div class="md-field md-theme-default md-focused md-has-value">
  <label for="card-number">16 digit card number</label>
  <input class="md-input" id="card-number" type="text">
/div>
```

When inspecting in IE11, I get an exception in the MdInput code, which I presume is the cause:

    if (value.constructor.name.toLowerCase() !== 'inputevent') {
    -> Unable to get property 'toLowerCase' of undefined or null reference



Using beta-7
pr_title: fix: constructor name support for IE11
app_description: Material Design for Vue.js

issue_title: [BUG] `DataGrid` filter input drops characters on server-side filtering
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

While typing in a DataGrid column filter (server-side mode) the input loses the most recent characters after the fetch completes. The filter still works, but the text field partially resets, which makes typing unreliable.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ea853f91-178b-4d05-81d3-fd4ec795385e

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Run the examples/finefoods-material-ui app.
2. Go to the Orders list page.
3. Open a column filter panel (filter icon / column menu).
4. Type quickly in a text filter (e.g. customer or store).
5. After the request triggers, observe the input losing some of the last typed characters.

### Expected behavior

The filter input should keep the full typed value while the server request is triggered.

### Packages

#### System:
 - OS: macOS 26.1
 - CPU: (10) arm64 Apple M2 Pro

 - Node: 22.14.0 - ~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.14.0/bin/node

issue_title: [BUG]  `<DataTableFilterDropdownText />` doesn't respect `defaultOperator`
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When the [`<DataTableFilterDropdownText />`](https://github.com/refinedev/refine/blob/main/packages/refine-ui/registry/new-york/refine-ui/data-table/data-table-filter.tsx) opens with a default operator (for example `contains`), the first request still uses `eq` as a value.

### Steps To Reproduce

### Expected behavior

The first request should use the default operator that shows in the UI. No manual change should be needed.

issue_title: [BUG] MUI useModalForm() can't edit the same resource ID back to back
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

A edit dialog implemented with useModalForm() will work well if every time I tried to open to edit a different resource id, but will always fail to populate the data if I open the same resource as the last-open one.

### Steps To Reproduce

I am using the MUI useModalForm() in a resource list page to to edit selected resource by users. I created a Edit dialog component, and embedded a single instance at the end of the page.

- When I open the modal (action='edit') the first time on a resource id, it was able to read the resource and populate the form with correct data.
- When I opened the modal on the same resource id again (back to back), it failed to populate the form data. All the fields are empty.
- If I tried to open a "different" resource id than the last-open id, it started working again. 
- Note: the isDirty state is also not reset between different edit sessions, and it is not consistent with the dirtyFields value.

### Expected behavior

I expect that open to edit the same resource back-to-back will still work.

### Packages

"@refinedev/core": "^4.57.9",
"@refinedev/mui": "^6.2.1",
"@refinedev/nextjs-router": "^6.2.3",
"@refinedev/react-hook-form": "^4.10.2",

### Additional Context

_No response_
pr_title: fix(react-hook-form): fix init. of `useModalForm` form values in back-to-back edit

issue_title: [BUG] useGetToPath is not working
issue_body: ### Describe the bug useGetToPath always returns undefined. Documentation is also wrong. https://refine.dev/docs/routing/hooks/use-get-to-path/#usage At docs > getToPath({ resource: This doesn't accept string like the docs says so, it should be an object like {name: "blog_posts"} ### Steps To Reproduce 1. npm create refine-app@latest refine-test 2. ✔ What would you like to name your project?: · refine-test ✔ Choose your backend service to connect: · data-provider-custom-json-rest (REST) ✔ Do you want to use a UI Framework?: · antd ✔ Do you want to add example pages?: · yes ✔ Do you need any Authentication logic?: · none 3. open src/pages/categories/list.tsx 4. add a button before Table comp. 5. define hooks. ```typescript const getToPath = useGetToPath() const go = useGo() ``` 6. define the handler to be called upon clicking the button ```typescript const clickHandler = () => { const path = getToPath({ resource: { name: "blog_posts" }, action: "edit", meta: { id: "1" } }) console.log(path) go({ to: path }) } ``` path is always undefined, there is no way I could get a meaningful value. So it doesn't redirect you to the intended path. It works perfect if you type your path without this hook with pure string at go func. Like: ```typescript go({ to: normal stringPath, not getToPath() }) ``` ### Expected behavior getToPath should return a string to be passed to ```go({to: })``` key. ### Packages ## System: - OS: macOS 15.7.2 - CPU: (8) arm64 Apple M2 ## Binaries: - Node: 22.12.0 - /Users/bugraotken/.nvm/versions/node/v22.12.0/bin/node - Yarn: Not Found - npm: 10.9.0 - /Users/bugraotken/.nvm/versions/node/v22.12.0/bin/npm ## Browsers: - Chrome: 143.0.7499.42 - Firefox: 145.0.2 - Safari: 26.1 ## Refine Packages: - @refinedev/antd: 6.0.3 - @refinedev/cli: 2.16.50 - @refinedev/core: 5.0.6 - @refinedev/devtools: 2.0.3 - @refinedev/kbar: 2.0.1 - @refinedev/react-router: 2.0.3 - @refinedev/simple-rest: 6.0.1 ### Additional Context _No response_
pr_title: fix(core): `useGetToPath` returns `undefined`

issue_title: [BUG] `queryOptions.select` runs multiple times
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I'm using GraphQL and I'm changing the data with the React Query's `select` middleware. It should run only once per request, but it is running multiple times. 

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Use the `useList` hook
2. Add `queryOptions.select: (data) => { console.log('select'); return data }`
3. See "select" is logged multiple times

Replication CodeSandbox: https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/react-query-5-forked-hh85pv

### Expected behavior

It should run only once. In React Query's documentation, they say to memoize the select function: https://tanstack.com/query/latest/docs/framework/react/guides/render-optimizations#memoization

`useList` is changing the function reference on every render.

### Packages

- "@refinedev/core": "^4.54.0",

### Additional Context

Possible fix: pass the `select` function inside a `useCallback` to `useList`'s `useQuery`
pr_title: fix(core): memoize queryOptions.select in useList hook

issue_title: [BUG] Multiple Filters not Supported by Supabase Realtime
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Right now, Supabase Realtime does not support multiple filters. If used, the live provider for supabase will send an invalid filter payload, causing the realtime request to fail.

### Steps To Reproduce

1. Create any realtime resource
2. Add more than 1 filter to the resource (I was using a useList hook with multiple objects in the filters option)
3. Watch websocket network requests, it will show an error.

### Expected behavior

I think we should either log a warning if we see multiple filters, or naively take the first one? 

### Packages

- supabase realtime v2.30.34 (not latest, but also not fixed in latest)
- @refinedev/supabase 5.9.4

### Additional Context

See here for Refine's implementation, where it is joining each filter with a comma. 

https://github.com/refinedev/refine/blame/8be95d808ae2f3f280ebd015bd95eb04238fe463/packages/supabase/src/liveProvider/index.ts#L67

See here for feature that is being discussed. No real activity it seems.

https://github.com/supabase/realtime/issues/486
https://github.com/supabase/realtime-js/issues/97

issue_title: [BUG] Creating a new Note with an existing Project fails to save the project assignment
issue_body:
### Description
When creating a new Note and trying to add an existing Project to it, the project selection appears to work and shows "Unsaved changes" notification, but when the Note is saved, it fails and the Project does not get added to the Note.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Navigate to the Notes view or open a Project details page
2. Click the "New Note" button or "+" icon to create a new Note
3. Enter a title for the Note (e.g., "Test Note")
4. Click the folder/project icon at the bottom to expand the project selection section
5. Search for and select an existing Project from the dropdown
6. Observe the "Unsaved changes" notification appears
7. Click the "Save" or "Create Note" button
8. Observe that the save operation fails
9. The Project is not associated with the Note after creation

### Expected Behavior
When creating a new Note and selecting an existing Project from the dropdown, the Project should be successfully associated with the Note when saved. The Note should be created with the `project_id`/`project_uid` properly set, and when viewing the Note, it should show the associated Project.

### Actual Behavior
1. User creates a new Note
2. User selects an existing Project from the project dropdown
3. "Unsaved changes" notification appears (indicating the UI detected the change)
4. User clicks Save
5. The save operation fails (backend returns an error or the Project is not attached)
6. The Note is created but without the Project association, or the Note creation fails entirely

### Operating System
macOS

### Browser
Chrome

### Tududi Version
Latest (main branch)

### Screenshots
N/A

### Additional Context
**Technical Details:**
The issue is in `frontend/utils/notesService.ts`. The `createNote` function (lines 23-33) does not transform the project data like `updateNote` does (lines 39-51).

When a project is selected in NoteModal (`frontend/components/Note/NoteModal.tsx` line 350-358), it sets:
- `formData.project_uid` = project.uid
- `formData.project` = nested object with {id, name, uid}

However, `createNote` sends the data as-is without ensuring `project_uid` is properly extracted from the nested `project` object if needed.

In contrast, `updateNote` has transformation logic:
```typescript
if (noteData.project && noteData.project.uid) {
    requestData.project_uid = noteData.project.uid;
}
```

But `createNote` lacks this transformation, causing inconsistency between create and update operations.

**Impact:**
Users cannot add Projects to new Notes during creation, breaking the expected workflow for organizing notes within projects. This forces users to create the Note first, then edit it to add the Project, which is cumbersome and unintuitive.
pr_title: Fix note creation with project assignment
app_description: Self-hosted task management with hierarchical organization, multi-language support, and Telegram integration.

issue_title: [BUG] Monthly recurring tasks don’t show next occurrence date in task list views
issue_body:
### Description

When creating a monthly recurring task and viewing it in **any task list view**, the monthly recurring tasks are displayed without any **date information**. The recurrence label (“Monthly”) appears, but users cannot see when the **next occurrence** is scheduled. They must click on the task to access this information, which interrupts the workflow.

Adding the date directly next to the “Monthly” label would introduce visual clutter and break Tududi’s minimalist layout. A better solution is to provide contextual information through a **tooltip/title** showing the **next occurrence date** when the user mouse hovers over the monthky recurrence label.

This approach preserves the clean UI while giving users the essential information they need.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a task with **Monthly** recurrence.
2. Open **any task list view**: All Tasks.
3. Locate the monthly recurring task.
4. Notice that the **next occurrence date** is not displayed.

### Expected Behavior

* The next occurrence date should be **easily accessible**, ideally via a title/tooltip on mouse hover.
* The UI should remain minimalist and uncluttered.

### Actual Behavior

Users have to click on the task to get the next occurence of the recurring taks, breaking their workflow.

### Operating System

Linux

### Browser

Chrome

### Tududi Version

0.86.1

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

issue_title: [BUG] Newly created tags missing UID in store, causing "Error fetching tag" on click
issue_body:
**AI-disclaimer**: I discovered this bug & asked Opus to investigate it. It created this write-up & the suggested fix.


### Description

When a new tag is created through the note editor (or task editor), clicking that tag in the Tags list page shows "Error fetching tag" until the page is refreshed.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open an existing note (or create one)
2. Add a new tag name (e.g., "new-tag") and save
3. Navigate to the Tags section in the sidebar
4. Hover over the newly created tag — the link shows `/tag/new-tag` (no UID prefix)
5. Click it → **"Error fetching tag"**
6. Refresh the page
7. Hover again — link now shows `/tag/<uid>-new-tag`
8. Click it → works correctly


### Expected Behavior

Clicking tag should display the note with that tag

### Actual Behavior

Error fetching tag

### Operating System

Windows

### Browser

Firefox

### Tududi Version

v0.88.5

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Additional Context

I asked Opus to dig into the code and pinpoint the bug. It generated this report + suggested a fix:

## Summary

When a new tag is created through the note editor (or task editor), clicking that tag in the Tags list page shows "Error fetching tag" until the page is refreshed.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open an existing note (or create one)
2. Add a new tag name (e.g., "new-tag") and save
3. Navigate to the Tags section in the sidebar
4. Hover over the newly created tag — the link shows `/tag/new-tag` (no UID prefix)
5. Click it → **"Error fetching tag"**
6. Refresh the page
7. Hover again — link now shows `/tag/<uid>-new-tag`
8. Click it → works correctly

## Root Cause

In `frontend/store/useStore.ts` line 310-321, `addNewTags()` adds tags to the Zustand store with a temporary `id` but **no `uid`**:

```js
addNewTags: (newTagNames: string[]) => {
    // ...
    const tagsToAdd = newTagNames
        .filter((name) => !existingTagNames.includes(name))
        .map((name) => ({ name, id: Date.now() + Math.random() })); // Temporary ID — no uid!
    // ...
}
```

The tag is created server-side when the note/task is saved (backend auto-creates tags from the `tags` array), but the store is never updated with the server response containing the real `uid`.

Later, in `frontend/components/Tags.tsx` line 227, the tag URL is generated:

```jsx
tag.uid
    ? `/tag/${tag.uid}-${tag.name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/^-|-$/g, '')}`
    : `/tag/${encodeURIComponent(tag.name)}`
```

Since `uid` is undefined, it falls back to `/tag/new-tag`. When `TagDetails.tsx` tries to load this, `extractUidFromSlug("new-tag")` returns `"new-tag"` (not a valid UID), `fetchTagBySlug` calls `GET /api/tag?uid=new-tag`, the backend returns 404, and the catch block shows the generic error.

## Suggested Fix

After saving a note/task that includes new tags, refresh the tags in the global store from the server response or re-fetch tags. For example, in `addNewTags()`:

```js
addNewTags: async (newTagNames: string[]) => {
    const sta
pr_title: Fix tag links for newly created tags (fixes #842)

issue_title: [BUG] Weekly progress chart shows blue bars for 0 tasks and lacks visible counts
issue_body: ### Description In the Today tab, the **Weekly progress** chart has two UX issues: 1. **Days with 0 tasks completed still display a blue column**, which visually suggests that something was completed on that day even though the value is zero. 2. The **number of tasks completed is only visible on hover**, not directly on the chart. This forces the user to either hover each column or mentally estimate the value from the Y-axis, which is unnecessary cognitive load. Fixing both points would make the chart more accurate and immediately useful as a quick weekly summary. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to the **Today** tab. 2. Ensure you have at least one day in the current week where **0 tasks** were completed. 3. Look at the **Weekly progress** chart. 4. Hover over different columns to see the tooltip with the number of completed tasks: there is some blue for the**0 tasks** day. ### Expected Behavior * For days where **0 tasks** were completed, the chart should **not show a blue bar** at all, so it’s immediately clear that nothing was completed that day. * Each column should **display the number of tasks completed directly inside the bar** so users can instantly read the values **without hovering** and without having to estimate from the Y-axis. Displaying "0" might be unecessary. ### Actual Behavior * Days with **0 completed tasks** still show a **blue column**, which is visually misleading. * The **exact count** of completed tasks is only available via **hover tooltip**, requiring extra interaction and mental effort to interpret the chart. ### Operating System Linux ### Browser Chrome ### Tududi Version 0.86.1 ### Screenshots _No response_ ### Additional Context _No response_

### Description

  Cannot change project assignment for tasks via web UI. The project dropdown and the Save button do not

  1. Open any task in the web interface

  3. Click on the project folder icon
  4. Select a new project (first time) or a different project (change project)

  6. Observe: No change is saved 

### Expected Behavior

  - Task should be assigned to the selected project
  - A PATCH request to `/api/task/{id}` should be sent with `project_id` parameter (?)

### Actual Behavior

  - No PATCH request is sent to the API
  - Only GET requests with 304 status visible in network tab  (? please check)
  - Task project assignment remains unchanged 

### Operating System

macOS

### Browser

Chrome

### Tududi Version

0.86-rc.1, 0.86-rc.3

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Additional Context

  **What DOES Work:**
  - Changing labels ✓
  - Changing priority ✓
  - Changing due date ✓
  - Updating project via direct API call ✓

  **What Does NOT Work:**
  - Project dropdown in web UI ✗

  **Workaround:**
  Use direct API call:
  ```bash
  curl -X PATCH https://todo.example.com/api/task/36 \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"project_id": 27}'
```

issue_title: [BUG] Canceled edits still show previous values when re-editing until page refresh
issue_body:
### Description

When editing a task in a project that initially has **no due date**, selecting a **Due Date** and then **canceling** the edit still leaves the previously selected date visible when the task is re-edited.
After refreshing the page, the field correctly appears empty again.

The same behavior occurs for **other field values as well** (e.g., priority, recurrence, etc.): if you make a change and then cancel, the previous selection remains visible when re-editing until the entire page is refreshed.
This suggests that the canceled edits are not properly reverted in the UI state.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Edit a task in a project that has **no due date**.
2. Select a **Due Date**.
3. **Cancel** the edit.
4. Re-edit the same task → the previously selected due date is still shown.
5. **Refresh the browser page.**
6. Edit the same task again → the due date is now correctly empty.
7. Repeat the same test with other editable fields (e.g., priority, recurrence) → same issue occurs.

### Expected Behavior

Canceling the edit should discard all unsaved changes, and all field values should revert to their previous state immediately, without requiring a page refresh.

### Actual Behavior

Field values (e.g., Due Date, priority, recurrence) remain visible after canceling and re-editing, even though they were never saved.
They only revert correctly after refreshing the page.

### Operating System

Linux

### Browser

Chrome

### Tududi Version

0.86

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_
pr_title: Revert state of option selections
app_description: Self-hosted task management with hierarchical organization, multi-language support, and Telegram integration.

issue_title: [BUG] UX/UI Regression Low and None priorities use same color — needs visual distinction
issue_body: ### Description Version 0.86 introduced a fourth priority level: **None**, which is now the default for newly created tasks. However, both **None** and **Low** priorities currently share the same **grey** color, making them visually indistinguishable. This is a UI/UX regression as prior to the addition of **None**, each priority level was clearly differentiated by color (High = red, Medium = orange, Low = grey). The new **None** level breaks that visual clarity, as users can no longer tell at a glance whether a task is intentionally set to **Low** or simply left at **None**. Prioritizing tasks is at the core of Tududi, and clear visual differentiation is essential for maintaining quick scanning and intuitive task management. Current situation: ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Create a new task in a Project (priority defaults to **None**). 2. Create another task and manually set its priority to **Low**. 3. Observe both tasks in list view!: both appear with the same **grey** color. ### Expected Behavior Each priority level should be visually distinct to preserve quick visual scanning of task importance. Introduce a **distinct color** for **Low** priority tasks, for example: * **Low**→ **Blue** (calm, low importance) Keep: * **None** → **Grey** (neutral, unset) Proposition **Blue** for **Low**: ### Actual Behavior Both **None** and **Low** task priorities appear grey, causing confusion and loss of visual clarity. ### Operating System Linux ### Browser Chrome ### Tududi Version 0.86 ### Screenshots _No response_ ### Additional Context _No response_
pr_title: Add blue to low priority tasks
app_description: Self-hosted task management with hierarchical organization, multi-language support, and Telegram integration.

issue_title: [Bug]: Jellyfin session bugs out when opening Feishin and the Jellyfin server is down
issue_body: ### I have already checked through the existing bug reports and found no duplicates - [x] Yes ### App Version 1.0.1-beta.1 ### Music Server and Version Jellyfin v10.11.5 ### What local environments are you seeing the problem on? Desktop Linux, Desktop Windows ### What happened? First of all, I could only test this with a Jellyfin server, I don't know if the bug replicates to other media servers. I found a weird issue that happens when the media server is unavailable in the beta release. Here is what I see when restarting Feishin after killing my Jellyfin server: The UI is stuck in a weird middle state, where it wants me to login, but it does have a server configured. Even if I bring the Jellyfin server back online, the issue remains even after restarting Feishin. The only way to fix this is to configure the server again. After configuring the server, you will be able to switch back to the original configuration, but you will get the following error: Notice that the server list is now available at the top of the screen. Attempting to login while in this state will lead to the same "Your session has expired" error. The only fix is to delete the old profile and use the new one. I will try and look into the code for a little, maybe I can come up with a fix. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Setup a Jellyfin server inside Feishin 2. Kill the Jellyfin server 3. Restart Feishin 4. You will be sent back to the login screen, with no server list at the top 5. You will have to setup your server again, after which the player will work 6. Switching back to the old server will return the error: "Your session has expired". This issue persists even when trying to log back in ### Relevant log output ```shell ```

diff --git a/rules/jotai-testing.md b/rules/jotai-testing.md
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..aaec3ecd8
--- /dev/null
+++ b/rules/jotai-testing.md
@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+# Jotai Testing
+
+Learnings for writing unit tests against components/hooks that read or write Jotai atoms.
+
+## Sharing a store across `renderHook` calls in a single test
+
+When a test needs to render a hook, unmount it, and then render the hook again (e.g., to verify state persists across an unmount/remount — the exact scenario for atoms that replace local `useState`), all `renderHook` calls must share the **same Jotai store**. Otherwise each `renderHook`'s `Provider` wrapper creates its own isolated store and writes made by the first hook are invisible to the second.
+
+**Wrong** — each call to `makeWrapper()` returns a component that creates a fresh `<Provider>` (no store prop), so every `renderHook` gets a new default store:
+
+```tsx
+function makeWrapper() {
+  return function Wrapper({ children }) {
+    return <Provider>{children}</Provider>;
+  };
+}
+```
+
+**Right** — create one store per test and bind every `renderHook` in that test to it:
+
+```tsx
+import { createStore, Provider } from "jotai";
+
+function makeWrapper() {
+  const store = createStore();
+  return function Wrapper({ children }) {
+    return <Provider store={store}>{children}</Provider>;
+  };
+}
+
+// In the test:
+const wrapper = makeWrapper();
+const first = renderHook(() => useMyAtomHook(id), { wrapper });
+// ... mutate state ...
+first.unmount();
+const second = renderHook(() => useMyAtomHook(id), { wrapper });
+// second now sees state written by first
+```
+
+The symptom when you get this wrong is assertions like `expected false to be true` on the remounted hook's state, even though the setter clearly ran against the first hook.
+
+See `src/atoms/githubSyncAtoms.test.tsx` for a complete example covering unmount/remount, cross-unmount completion, and per-key isolation.
diff --git a/src/atoms/githubSyncAtoms.test.tsx b/src/atoms/githubSyncAtoms.test.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 0000

**Describe the bug**
In App A start a prompt and leave it unsent and unqueued. Click into App B and the prompt will disappear. Click back into App A's prompt that had the unsent and unqueued message in it and it is deleted. No way to recover it. 

**To Reproduce**
See above

**Expected behavior**
Should still be there.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: Windows 11 Pro
- Browser: Chrome
- Version Latest Beta of Dyad

**Additional context**
None

+import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
+import {

+  maybeCaptureRetryReplayText,
+  toToolResultOutput,
+} from "@/pro/main/ipc/handlers/local_agent/retry_replay_utils";
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// buildRetryReplayMessages
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+describe("buildRetryReplayMessages", () => {
+  it("returns empty array when no events are provided", () => {
+    expect(buildRetryReplayMessages([])).toEqual([]);
+  });
+
+  it("replays a single completed tool exchange", () => {
+    const events: RetryReplayEvent[] = [
+      { type: "assistant-text", text: "Let me read that file." },
+      {
+        type: "tool-call",
+        toolCallId: "call-1",
+        toolName: "readFile",
+        input: { path: "foo.ts" },
+      },
+      {
+        type: "tool-result",
+        toolCallId: "call-1",
+        toolName: "readFile",
+        output: "file contents",
+      },
+    ];
+
+    const messages = buildRetryReplayMessages(events);
+    expect(messages).toHaveLength(2);
+    expect(messages[0].role).toBe("assistant");
+    expect(messages[0].content).toEqual([
+      { type: "text", text: "Let me read that file." },
+      {
+        type: "tool-call",
+        toolCallId: "call-1",
+        toolName: "readFile",
+        input: { path: "foo.ts" },
+      },
+    ]);
+    expect(messages[1].role).toBe("tool");
+    expect(messages[1].content).toEqual([
+      {
+        type: "tool-result",

+        toolName: "readFile",
+        output: { type: "text", value: "file contents" },
+      },
+    ]);
+  });

pr_title: fix: increase tool call limit to 50 and show clear pause message
diff_preview:
diff --git a/e2e-tests/fixtures/engine/local-agent/step-limit.ts b/e2e-tests/fixtures/engine/local-agent/step-limit.ts
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..1f14e9700
--- /dev/null
+++ b/e2e-tests/fixtures/engine/local-agent/step-limit.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+import type {
+  LocalAgentFixture,
+  Turn,
+} from "../../../../testing/fake-llm-server/localAgentTypes";
+
+/**
+ * Fixture that triggers the step limit by generating 50 tool call turns.
+ * The AI SDK's stepCountIs(50) will stop after 50 steps, and the handler
+ * will append a <dyad-step-limit> notice to the response.
+ */
+const toolCallTurns: Turn[] = Array.from({ length: 50 }, (_, i) => ({
+  text: `Step ${i + 1}: reading file.`,
+  toolCalls: [
+    {
+      name: "read_file",
+      args: { path: "package.json" },
+    },
+  ],
+}));
+
+// Final text-only turn (won't be reached because stepCountIs(50) stops first)
+const finalTurn: Turn = {
+  text: "All steps completed.",
+};
+
+export const fixture: LocalAgentFixture = {
+  description:
+    "Triggers step limit by making 50+ tool call rounds, causing a pause notification",
+  turns: [...toolCallTurns, finalTurn],
+};

+ * a <dyad-step-limit> notification card.

+
+testSkipIfWindows("local-agent - step limit pause", async ({ po }) => {
+  await po.setUpDyadPro({ localAgent: true });
+  await po.importApp("minimal");
+  await po.chatActions.selectLocalAgentMode();
+
+  await po.sendPrompt("tc=local-agent/step-limit");
+
+  // Verify the step limit card is visible
+  await expect(
+    po.page.getByText("Paused after 50 tool calls", { exact: true }),
+  ).toBeVisible({
+    timeout: Timeout.EXTRA_LONG,
+  });
+
+  // Verify the "Continue" button is shown
+  await expect(po.page.getByRole("button", { name: "Continue" })).toBeVisible({
+    timeout: Timeout.MEDIUM,
+  });
+
+  // Click the "Continue" button
+  await po.page.getByRole("button", { name: "Continue" }).click();
+
+  await po.snapshotMessages();
+});

issue_title: Custom Date Field Doesn’t Show DatePicker on New Ticket Form
issue_body:
### Information about bug


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/328c34a0-c9e8-481a-874e-12388297f414

**Description:**
Added a custom field `due_date` with FieldType **Date** and added it to the **HD Ticket Template**.
When creating a new ticket, the field shows as a plain text input instead of a DatePicker.

**Expected:**
`due_date` should render as a DatePicker to allow easy date selection.

**Actual:**
Field renders as a text input, requiring users to manually type the date.

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Create custom field `due_date` with type = Date
2. Add it to HD Ticket Template Fields
3. Open “Create New Ticket” form

**Result:**
No DatePicker displayed for the field.

--



### Version

Frappe Helpdesk Latest Version

### Installation method

docker

### Relevant log output / Stack trace / Full Error Message.

```shell

```

issue_title: Ellipsis 更新后出现的bug
issue_body:
### Version of antd-mobile

5.38.1

### Operating system and its version

Others

### Browser and its version

Google Chrome

### Sandbox to reproduce

https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/practical-wu-74vqhm

### What happened?

### 问题
#### 1. 文本省略字数错误
5.37.1时 渲染符合预期
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/14fa4cca-8999-496e-b597-dcecda3180d5)
5.38.1时 被截取的文本过多
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fcb2116d-d699-4e77-97f5-08d1b50c217c)


#### 2. 特殊情况下，计算rowMeasureHeight值精度误差，导致省略行数错误
5.37.1时 渲染符合预期
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/af4cd519-db00-48be-bb4f-552de5bdc4f6)

5.38.1时 rows为2却渲染了一行
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f577a5c-7cc4-46f6-9f38-eda3091a9ffc)

### 分析
1. useLayoutEffect获取元素高度问题
第一次计算的时候页面还没有展示元素，此时计算为59<=40为false，进行了文本分割，然后页面对文本进行了渲染
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b6f7c56e-d5bc-4e77-bc2b-1830815951f7)。
第二次计算由requestAnimationFrame调用startMeasure触发计算，此时计算获取的元素节点还是未分割的文本的元素，
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/36258dc6-7d81-4e71-9a60-f3f5e1da0302)
所以高度获取的时候还是59<=40，导致再一次进行了分割，所以出现文本被截断过多。

2. rowMeasureHeight计算精度误差
由于获取的是offsetHeight，获取的值会进行四舍五入，例如一行为 16.4px，两行时是32.8px，则会变为 33<=16*2命中false逻辑导致重新修剪文本，这种情况在移动端的rem转换下会比较容易出现。

### 可能解法
1. 第一个问题比较奇怪，不知道为什么会出现页面渲染是新的，但是元素节点还是旧的。暂时没有想到比较好的解法。

3. 加上0.5的误差处理，之前的Ellipsis的实现中就有rows+0.5的处理。或者使用getComputedStyle获取高度，可以保留小数位。




### Relevant log output

_No response_
